title: Decree on the approval of technical roadworthiness and on the technical conditions for the operation of vehicles on roads

parts.JEDNOTLIVÁ VÝROBA A PŘESTAVBA SILNIČNÍHO: INDIVIDUAL PRODUCTION AND CONVERSION OF ROAD VEHICLES
parts.SPOLEČNÁ USTANOVENÍ, ZRUŠOVACÍ USTANOVEN: COMMON PROVISIONS, REPEALING PROVISIONS AND EFFECTIVE DATE
parts.TECHNICKÉ ZKUŠEBNY A ZKUŠEBNÍ STANICE: TECHNICAL TESTING LABORATORIES AND TESTING STATIONS
parts.ZÁKLADNÍ ZNAKY TYPU A KATEGORIE VOZIDEL: BASIC CHARACTERISTICS OF VEHICLE TYPES AND CATEGORIES

p1.heading: Subject matter
p1.text:
(1) This Decree shall apply to legal relations in the field of technical roadworthiness approval and to the technical conditions for the operation of vehicles on roads, which are not regulated by a directly applicable European Union regulation governing the approval of technical roadworthiness of vehicles of categories L, T, C and R^1).

(2) A list of the full names of the European Union directives used in this Decree, including the numerical designation of the basic directive and the numerical designation of the latest amending legal regulation, is provided in Annex No. 1 to this Decree.

p2.heading: Basic concepts
p2.text:
For the purposes of this Decree, the following shall mean:

- a) „towing vehicle“ a motor vehicle coupled with a trailer,

- b) „vehicle combination“ the coupling of a towing vehicle with one or more trailers,

- c) „demountable body“ a separate technical unit that is detachably connected to the base vehicle, a carrier of demountable bodies,

- d) „harmonised type approval“ the type approval of a road vehicle, its system, component or separate technical unit to which the relevant European Union regulations apply,

- e) „international type approval“ the procedure for the approval of a vehicle system, component or separate technical unit established in accordance with decisions adopted on the basis of the Agreement concerning the Adoption of Uniform Technical Prescriptions for Wheeled Vehicles, Equipment and Parts which can be Fitted and/or be Used on Wheeled Vehicles and the Conditions for Reciprocal Recognition of Approvals Granted on the Basis of these Prescriptions, concluded in Geneva on 20 March 1958, as amended by the Agreement of 16 October 1995,

- f) „non-harmonised type approval“ the type approval of a road vehicle, its system, component or separate technical unit to which neither the relevant European Union regulations nor an international treaty in the field of technical roadworthiness, by which the Czech Republic is bound, apply,

- g) „vehicle identification number“ a specific number of characters, numbers and letters, which are specific to the given vehicle, its composition and use are determined by the relevant European Union regulations,

- h) „technical protocol“ a document prepared by a technical testing laboratory or testing station, confirming that the vehicle, its systems, components or separate technical units, which were submitted to these entities for tests or verifications, comply with the data specified in the relevant document containing technical data or in the approval documentation and meet the relevant technical, harmonised or international requirements; the technical protocol also states the measured values,

- i) „maximum permissible mass“ the maximum mass with which the vehicle may be used in operation on roads in the Czech Republic,

- j) „maximum permissible axle mass“ the maximum axle mass with which the vehicle may be used in operation on roads in the Czech Republic,

- k) „maximum technically permissible axle mass“ the mass corresponding to the maximum technically permissible static vertical load exerted by the vehicle axle on the road surface,

- l) „maximum technically permissible laden vehicle mass“ the maximum mass determined for the vehicle based on its design characteristics and execution; the maximum technically permissible mass of a trailer or semi-trailer includes the static mass transferred to the towing vehicle, if the trailer or semi-trailer is attached,

- m) „maximum technically permissible laden vehicle combination mass“ the maximum mass determined for a combination of a motor vehicle and one or more trailers based on its design characteristics and execution or the maximum mass determined for a vehicle combination consisting of a semi-trailer tractor and a semi-trailer,

- n) „instantaneous mass of a vehicle or vehicle combination“ the mass ascertained at a specific moment during their operation on roads,

- o) „mass in running order“

- 1. for motor vehicles:

the mass of the vehicle with its fuel tank filled to at least 90% of its capacity, including the mass of the driver, fuel and liquids, equipped with standard equipment according to the manufacturer's specifications, and, if part of the equipment, also the mass of the bodywork, cabin, coupling device and spare wheel, as well as tools,

- 2. in the case of a trailer:

the mass of the vehicle, including fuel and liquids, equipped with standard equipment according to the manufacturer's specifications, and, if part of the equipment, also the mass of the bodywork, additional coupling device and spare wheel and tools.

p3.heading: Basic characteristics for determining vehicle types, variants and versions for individual vehicle categories
p3.text:
(To § 2 (10) of the Act)

(1) The basic characteristics for determining types, variants and versions of road vehicles, during harmonised type approval, for vehicles

- a) of categories M, N and O, are listed in Annex II Part B of Directive 2007/46/EC establishing a framework for the approval of motor vehicles and their trailers, and of systems, components and separate technical units intended for such vehicles, as amended, (hereinafter referred to as „Directive 2007/46/EC“),

- b) of category L are listed in the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the approval and market surveillance of two- or three-wheel vehicles and quadricycles^12),

- c) of categories T, C, R and S are listed in the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the approval and market surveillance of agricultural and forestry vehicles^13).

(2) For determining the basic characteristics of types, variants and versions of road vehicles of category Z, paragraph 1 shall apply mutatis mutandis.

p4.heading: Basic characteristics for determining types of systems, components and separate technical units
p4.text:
(To § 2 para. 11 of the Act)

(1) The basic characteristics for determining types of systems, components and separate technical units, when approving a harmonised type, for vehicles

- a) of categories M, N and O are listed in Art. 3 and Art. 10 of Directive 2007/46/EC,

- b) of category L are listed in the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the approval and market surveillance of two- or three-wheel vehicles and quadricycles^12),

- c) of categories T, C, R and S are listed in the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the approval and market surveillance of agricultural and forestry vehicles^13).

(2) The basic characteristics for determining types of systems, components and separate technical units to which an international treaty by which the Czech Republic is bound applies are listed in decisions adopted pursuant to the Agreement concerning the Adoption of Uniform Technical Prescriptions for Wheeled Vehicles, Equipment and Parts which can be Fitted and/or be Used on Wheeled Vehicles and the Conditions for Reciprocal Recognition of Approvals Granted on the Basis of these Prescriptions, concluded in Geneva on 20 March 1958, as amended by the Agreement of 16 October 1995 (hereinafter referred to as „decisions adopted pursuant to an international treaty“).

(3) For determining the basic characteristics of types of systems, components and separate technical units to which neither the relevant European Union regulations nor an international treaty in the field of technical fitness approval by which the Czech Republic is bound apply, paragraphs 1 and 2 shall apply mutatis mutandis.

p5.heading: Type of vehicle components and separate technical units that can be approved independently of the vehicle
p5.text:
(To § 2 para. 6 and 7 of the Act)

A type of component or separate technical unit may be approved independently of the vehicle only if it can be demonstrated that all technical, harmonised technical and international technical requirements stipulated for that type of separate technical unit or component are met without the separate technical unit or component being mounted on the vehicle.

p6.heading: Categories of road and special vehicles
p6.text:
(To § 2 para. 8 of the Act, § 3 para. 4 of the Act)

The description of categories of road and special vehicles, their further subdivision and the classification of vehicles into categories is provided in Annex No. 2 to this Decree.

p7.heading: Vehicles, systems, components and separate technical units to which the relevant European Union regulations do not apply
p7.text:
(To § 16 para. 9 of the Act)

The relevant European Union regulations shall not apply to

- a) road vehicles of categories M, N and O designed and manufactured for use by armed forces, civil defence, fire services and services responsible for maintaining public order,

- b) road vehicles of subcategory LZ,

- c) special vehicles of categories T4.1, T4.2, C, R and S, if the manufacturer requests approval of a non-harmonised type, and furthermore special vehicles of subcategories SS and SN,

- d) vehicles of category Z, or

- e) systems, components and separate technical units intended for vehicles according to letters a) to d).

p8.heading: Technical requirements for road vehicles, systems, components and separate technical units
p8.text:
(To § 16 para. 9 of the Act)

(1) For determining the technical requirements for road vehicles according to § 7 letter a), their systems, components and separate technical units, Annex No. 3 to this Decree and the directly applicable European Union regulations governing the approval of road vehicles of categories M, N and O shall apply mutatis mutandis. This does not apply to technical requirements whose application is hindered by operational and technical reasons related to the activity performed by the operators of these vehicles. For determining the technical requirements for road vehicles according to § 7 letter b), their systems, components and separate technical units, the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the approval and market surveillance of two- or three-wheel vehicles and quadricycles^12) shall apply mutatis mutandis. This does not apply to technical requirements whose application is hindered by operational and technical reasons related to the activity performed by the operators of these vehicles. For determining the technical requirements for road vehicles according to § 7 letter d), their systems, components and separate technical units, Annex No. 3 to this Decree and the directly applicable European Union regulations governing the approval of road vehicles of categories M, N and O, or the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the approval and market surveillance of two- or three-wheel vehicles and quadricycles^12) shall apply mutatis mutandis.

(2) For determining the technical requirements for systems, components and separate technical units intended for road vehicles, according to § 7, Annex No. 5 to this Decree shall apply mutatis mutandis.

p9.heading: Harmonised technical requirements for vehicles, systems, components and separate technical units
p9.text:
(To § 16 para. 9 of the Act)

Harmonised technical requirements for road vehicles of categories M, N and O, systems, components and separate technical units intended for these vehicles are listed in Annex No. 3 to this Decree and in the directly applicable European Union regulations governing the approval of road vehicles of categories M, N and O.

p10.heading: International technical requirements for systems, components and separate technical units
p10.text:
(To § 16 para. 9 of the Act)

International technical requirements for systems, components and separate technical units are listed in decisions adopted pursuant to an international treaty. These decisions are listed in Annex No. 5 to this Decree.

p11.heading: Method of ensuring conformity of production of vehicles, their systems, components or separate technical units with the approved type
p11.text:
(To § 16 (9) of the Act)

(1) The method for ensuring the conformity of production of road vehicles, their systems, components or separate technical units with the approved type, when approving a harmonised type, for vehicles of categories M, N and O, is set out in Annex X to Directive 2007/46/EC.

(2) The method for ensuring the conformity of production of road vehicle systems, their components or separate technical units with the approved type when approving an international type is set out in decisions adopted on the basis of an international agreement.

p12.heading: Maximum number of vehicles in a small series
p12.text:
(To § 16 (9) of the Act)

The maximum number of road vehicles of categories M, N and O in a small series is set out in Annex XII to Directive 2007/46/EC.

p13.heading: Scope and content of technical documentation
p13.text:
(To § 17 (7) of the Act)

(1) The scope and content of technical documentation, when approving a harmonised type, for vehicles of categories M, N and O, their systems, components or separate technical units, are set out in Annex No. 3 to this Decree and in directly applicable European Union regulations governing the approval of vehicles of categories M, N and O.

(2) The scope and content of technical documentation when approving an international type are set out in decisions adopted on the basis of an international agreement.

p14.heading: Method of verifying conformity of production
p14.text:
(To § 19 (2) of the Act)

(1) The method of verifying the conformity of production of vehicles, their systems, components or separate technical units with the approved type, when approving a harmonised type, for vehicles of categories M, N and O, is set out in Annex X to Directive 2007/46/EC.

(2) The method of verifying the conformity of production of road vehicle systems, components or separate technical units with the approved type when approving an international type is set out in decisions adopted on the basis of an international agreement.

p15.heading: Particulars of the communication of the result of verification of conformity of production
p15.text:
(To § 19 (2) of the Act)

The communication on the result of verification of conformity of production must, for all vehicles of categories M, N and O subject to harmonised type approval, contain at least the data specified in Annex X to Directive 2007/46/EC.

p16.heading: Scope and method of verification of technical requirements, harmonised technical requirements and international technical requirements
p16.text:
(To § 20 (4) of the Act)

(1) The scope and method of verification of harmonised technical requirements, when approving a harmonised type, for vehicles of categories M, N and O, are set out in Annex No. 3 to this Decree and in directly applicable European Union regulations governing the approval of vehicles of categories M, N and O.

(2) The scope and method of verification of international technical requirements when approving an international type are set out in decisions adopted on the basis of an international agreement.

p17.heading: Method for determining the number of road vehicles, components or separate technical units for the purpose of verifying technical requirements, harmonised technical requirements and international technical requirements
p17.text:
(To § 20 (4) of the Act)

(1) The number of road vehicles of categories M, N and O and vehicles pursuant to § 7, components or separate technical units for the purpose of verifying technical requirements and harmonised technical requirements shall always be determined in such a way as to enable all tests to be carried out on the vehicle, component or separate technical unit that are necessary to verify compliance with the technical requirements or harmonised technical requirements to the extent that they have not yet been verified by tests already carried out.

(2) The method for determining the number of vehicles, components or separate technical units for the purpose of verifying international technical requirements when approving an international type is set out in decisions adopted on the basis of an international agreement.

p18.heading: Type-approval certificate
p18.text:
(To § 21 (4) of the Act)

(1) The template for the type-approval certificate when approving a harmonised type for vehicles of categories M, N and O, their systems, components or separate technical units, is set out in Annex No. 3 to this Decree and in directly applicable European Union regulations governing the approval of vehicles of categories M, N and O.

(2) The template for the type-approval certificate, when approving an international type, is set out in decisions adopted on the basis of an international agreement.

(3) The type-approval certificate when approving a non-harmonised type is a special form with protective features of A4 format, which contains data on the person to whom this certificate was issued, on the type of vehicle, type of system, type of component and type of separate technical unit for which this certificate was issued.

(4) The numbering system for harmonised type-approval certificates for vehicles of categories M, N and O is set out in Annex VII to Directive 2007/46/EC.

(5) Annex No. 7 to this Decree sets out

- a) the template for the type-approval certificate when approving a non-harmonised type,

- b) the template for the technical certificate of a road vehicle not subject to registration,

- c) the template for the technical certificate of a separate technical unit.

p19.heading: Type approval documentation
p19.text:
(To § 21 (4) of the Act, § 24 (5) of the Act)

(1) The particulars of the list of type-approval documentation are:

- a) identification of the authority that issued the type-approval,

- b) stamp of the entity that issued the type-approval,

- c) date of issue,

- d) type-approval number,

- e) total number of pages,

- f) number of separate drawings,

- g) number of separate photographs, and

- h) in the case of an extension of type-approval, the reason.

(2) Revisions to the type-approval shall be clearly marked on each page of the approval documentation affected by the revision. The date of the change made must be indicated on these pages of the approval documentation. Revisions to the type-approval may also be indicated in new approval documentation, provided that the changes made are described in detail therein.

(3) In the case of an extension of type-approval, the new certificate of type-approval shall be assigned a sequential extension number following the sequential number of the previous extension.

(4) Paragraphs 1 to 3 shall apply only to road vehicles of categories M, N and O, vehicles pursuant to § 7, and systems, components and separate technical units of these vehicles.

(5) The particulars of the list of approval documentation and the method of extension and revision of type-approval for international type-approval are set out in decisions adopted on the basis of an international agreement.

p20.heading: Declaration of Conformity
p20.text:
(To § 23 (4) of the Act)

(1) A declaration of conformity, for harmonised type-approval, shall be issued for road vehicles of categories M, N and O.

(2) The particulars of the declaration of conformity and its duplicate for vehicles of categories M, N, O are set out in Article 18 and Annex IX of Directive 2007/46/EC.

p21.text:
(1) The type-approval mark shall be affixed to all road vehicles of categories M, N and O, their components and separate technical units, and is set out in Annex No. 3 to this Decree and in directly applicable European Union regulations governing the approval of road vehicles of categories M, N and O; this does not apply to road vehicles pursuant to § 7 letter a), their components and separate technical units.

(2) The type-approval mark for international type-approval shall be affixed to all vehicle components and separate technical units of vehicles and is set out in decisions adopted on the basis of an international agreement.

(3) The type-approval mark for vehicles pursuant to § 7, their components and separate technical units shall be affixed to all vehicles, their components and separate technical units and is set out in Annex No. 8 to this Decree.

p22.heading: Maximum number of vehicles that may be placed on the market after the expiry of type-approval
p22.text:
(To § 26 (4) of the Act)

The maximum number of road vehicles of categories M, N, and O that may be placed on the market after the expiry of type-approval is set out in Annex XII of Directive 2007/46/EC.

p22a.heading: Approval of non-harmonised type
p22a.text:
(To § 16 (9) of the Act)

(1) For the approval of a non-harmonised type, the following shall apply mutatis mutandis:

- a) Annex No. 3 to this Decree and directly applicable European Union regulations governing the approval of road vehicles of categories M, N and O, if vehicles pursuant to § 7 letter a), their systems, components or separate technical units are being approved,

- b) directly applicable European Union regulation governing the approval of two- or three-wheel vehicles and quadricycles and market surveillance^12), if vehicles pursuant to § 7 letter b), their systems, components or separate technical units are being approved,

- c) directly applicable European Union regulation governing the approval of agricultural and forestry vehicles and market surveillance^13), if vehicles pursuant to § 7 letter c), their systems, components or separate technical units are being approved,

- d) Annex No. 3 to this Decree and directly applicable European Union regulations governing the approval of road vehicles of categories M, N and O, or directly applicable European Union regulation governing the approval of two- or three-wheel vehicles and quadricycles and market surveillance^12) or directly applicable European Union regulation governing the approval of agricultural and forestry vehicles and market surveillance^13), if vehicles pursuant to § 7 letter d), their systems, components or separate technical units are being approved.

(2) For the approval of non-harmonised type systems, components and separate technical units intended for vehicles pursuant to § 7, Annex No. 5 to this Decree shall also apply mutatis mutandis.

(3) The regulations pursuant to paragraphs 1 and 2 shall apply mutatis mutandis for determining:

- a) the method of ensuring the conformity of production of vehicles pursuant to § 7, their systems, components and separate technical units with the approved type,

- b) the scope and content of technical documentation,

- c) the method of verifying the conformity of production of vehicles pursuant to § 7, their systems, components and separate technical units with the approved type, and

- d) the scope and method of verifying technical requirements.

p23.heading: Technical testing stations
p23.text:
(To § 27 (6) of the Act)

(1) The categories of activities of technical services for vehicles of categories M, N and O, their systems, components and separate technical units are listed in Article 41 of Directive 2007/46/EC.

(2) The requirements for apparatus and other necessary technical equipment and for premises required for the performance of the activities of a technical service, the requirements for the internal organisation and management system, and the method for assessing compliance with these requirements for technical services inspecting vehicles of categories M, N and O, their systems, components and separate technical units are listed in Annex V of Directive 2007/46/EC.

(3) For the determination of the categories of activities of technical services, the requirements for their apparatus and other necessary technical equipment, the requirements for premises necessary for the performance of the activities of technical services, the requirements for the internal organisation and management system, and the method for assessing compliance with these requirements, the following shall apply mutatis mutandis:

- a) directly applicable European Union regulation governing the approval and market surveillance of two- or three-wheel vehicles and quadricycles^12), if verified:

- 1. during international type-approval, compliance with technical requirements by systems, components or separate technical units of vehicles of category L,

- 2. during non-harmonised type-approval, compliance with technical requirements by vehicles pursuant to § 7 letter b), their systems, components or separate technical units, or

- b) directly applicable European Union regulation governing the approval and market surveillance of agricultural and forestry vehicles^13), if verified:

- 1. during international type-approval, compliance with technical requirements by systems, components or separate technical units of vehicles of category T, C, R and S,

- 2. during non-harmonised type-approval, compliance with technical requirements by vehicles pursuant to § 7 letter c), their systems, components or separate technical units,

- c) Annex No. 3 to this Decree and directly applicable European Union regulations governing the approval of road vehicles of categories M, N and O, or directly applicable European Union regulation governing the approval and market surveillance of two- or three-wheel vehicles and quadricycles^12), or directly applicable European Union regulation governing the approval and market surveillance of agricultural and forestry vehicles^13), if compliance with technical requirements by vehicles pursuant to § 7 letter d), their systems, components or separate technical units is verified.

(4) The test report must comply with the requirements of Appendix 3 of Annex V of Directive 2007/46/EC.

p24.heading: Testing Stations
p24.text:
(To § 72 (3) of the Act)

(1) Testing stations are divided into:

- a) testing stations for road motor vehicles and trailers of categories L, M1, N1, O1 and O2,

- b) testing stations for road motor vehicles and trailers of categories M2, M3, N2, N3, O1, O2, O3 and O4 and special motor vehicles and trailers of categories T, C, R and S,

- c) testing stations for special motor vehicles and trailers of categories T, C, R and S,

- d) combined testing stations for motor vehicles and trailers according to letters a), b) and c) in various combinations according to vehicle categories,

- e) combined testing stations for motor vehicles and trailers according to letters a) and b) in various combinations according to vehicle categories in ADR design pursuant to the European Agreement concerning the International Carriage of Dangerous Goods by Road.

(2) Vehicles of category Z may be inspected by the testing station according to paragraph 1 that is capable of carrying out the inspection with regard to its technical equipment and parameters.

(3) The template for the authorisation form for a technical inspection station to perform technical inspections of individual types of vehicles and interchangeable superstructures or small series of vehicles before the approval of their technical roadworthiness and the template for the certificate for operating a testing station are provided in Annex No. 7 to this Decree.

p25.heading: Inspection Technician
p25.text:
(To § 72 (3) of the Act)

(1) Technical inspections of vehicles before the approval of their technical roadworthiness are carried out by a person who holds a valid professional certificate of an inspection technician and has obtained a certificate for carrying out technical inspections of vehicles before the approval of their technical roadworthiness (hereinafter referred to as “certificate”).

(2) A certificate may be obtained by an inspection technician who has completed a basic course to acquire professional competence for carrying out technical inspections of vehicles before the approval of their technical roadworthiness and has successfully passed an examination of this professional competence.

(3) To an inspection technician who has completed the basic course and passed the examination of professional competence according to paragraph 2 of this section (hereinafter referred to as “Type K inspection technician”), the Ministry of Transport (hereinafter referred to as “the Ministry”) shall issue a certificate and assign a stamp with a registration number. A graduate who failed the examination may individually supplement their knowledge and request a repeat commission examination. This request may be submitted no earlier than one month and no later than 6 months after the completion of the course. Further repetition of the examination is not possible without re-attending the basic course to acquire professional competence for carrying out technical inspections of vehicles before the approval of their technical roadworthiness.

(4) The holder of a certificate according to paragraph 3 shall undergo training in a refresher course and re-examination of professional competence for carrying out vehicle inspections before the approval of their technical roadworthiness once every 2 years, to the extent determined by the Ministry and published in the Transport Bulletin.

(5) The basic course, refresher course, examinations of professional competence according to paragraph 2, and re-examinations of professional competence according to paragraph 4 are carried out by the Ministry or a person authorised by it. The content of instruction in the basic course and refresher course and the method of conducting the examination and re-examination of professional competence shall be published by the Ministry in the Transport Bulletin.

(6) The template for the certificate is provided in Annex No. 7 to this Decree.

p26.heading: Type and amount of fee
p26.text:
(To § 87 of the Act)

(1) For training as a type K control technician in a basic course, the student shall pay the Ministry CZK 500.

(2) For training as a type K control technician in an advanced course, the student shall pay the Ministry CZK 200.

(3) For the final examination in both the basic and advanced courses, the student shall pay the Ministry CZK 100; for a repeated examination, CZK 200.

p27.heading: Electronic record of declarations of conformity and method of submitting technical data
p27.text:
[To § 28 para. 1 letter p), § 28d of the Act]

(1) The electronic record of declarations of conformity shall contain the following data:

- a) vehicle identification number,

- b) EC type-approval number,

- c) vehicle make,

- d) vehicle type,

- e) first name and surname of the person who issued the declaration of conformity, and

- f) date of issue of the declaration of conformity.

(2) The method and form of submitting data to the record of technical data of approved types of road vehicles are specified in Annex No. 9 to this Decree.

p27a.heading: Scope of tests performed by tachograph manufacturer
p27a.text:
[To § 28 para. 1 letter d) of the Act]

The manufacturer is obliged to perform tests on vehicle units and motion sensors in accordance with the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the construction, installation, use, testing and control of tachographs^14) at least once every 2 years.

p28.heading: Individual production of a road vehicle
p28.text:
(To § 31 para. 1, § 33 para. 2 of the Act)

(1) Technical requirements for individually manufactured vehicles are set out in Annex No. 10 to this Decree.

(2) The model certificate for individually manufactured road vehicles valid in all Member States is set out in Annex No. 7 to this Decree.

p29.heading: Vehicle conversion
p29.text:
(To § 74 para. 1, § 74 para. 2 of the Act)

(1) After conversion, a converted vehicle must meet the technical requirements that were valid at the time of its manufacture, or the technical requirements of later regulations listed in Annexes No. 3 to 6 of this Decree for the vehicle type of the relevant kind and category.

(2) If the vehicle identification number changes during vehicle conversion, the competent authority performing vehicle registration shall determine how the said number is marked on the new or changed structural or essential part of the vehicle mechanism. If the replacing structural part bears an identification number from another vehicle, this number shall always be invalidated by striking it with crosses so that it remains identifiable. The vehicle identification number of a vehicle where a structural or essential part of the vehicle mechanism is changed shall then be stamped near the invalidated number, provided that it is a structural part declared interchangeable by the manufacturer. Information on the stamping of the identification number using alternative technology shall be entered in the vehicle documents.

(3) In the event that the manufacturer declares an essential part of the mechanism or structure of a road vehicle to be a spare part for that vehicle, the procedure for stamping the vehicle identification number shall be analogous to that in paragraph 2.

(4) If a converted vehicle can be classified into multiple categories, its category shall be determined by the Ministry or the municipal authority of a municipality with extended powers, provided that the provisions of § 73 para. 4 of Act No. 56/2001 Sb., on the approval of technical roadworthiness and on the technical conditions for vehicle operation on roads, are not affected.

(5) In the case of vehicle conversion, including subsequent conversion, at least one original essential part of the vehicle mechanism or structure must be retained.

(6) Conditions for the conversion of vehicles powered by liquefied petroleum gas or compressed or liquefied natural gas or hydrogen are set out in Annex No. 11.

p31.heading: Requirements for the design and condition of equipment
p31.text:
(To § 76 para. 1 of the Act)

Annex No. 12 to this Decree sets out

- a) requirements for the design and condition of mandatory vehicle equipment, in Part B,

- b) technical requirements for the equipment of bicycles, animal-drawn vehicles and handcarts for their use in road traffic, in Part C,

- c) technical requirements for the equipment of vehicles with lighting devices, in Part D,

- d) technical requirements for the equipment of vehicles with special warning light and sound devices, in Part E,

- e) technical requirements for the equipment of vehicles with wheels, tyres and anti-skid chains, in Part F,

- f) technical requirements for the equipment of vehicles with supplementary devices, in Part G,

- g) technical requirements for the equipment of vehicles with maximum permitted speed markings, in Part H,

- h) technical requirements for the equipment of special vehicles, in Part I.

p32.heading: Equipment whose technical roadworthiness is approved
p32.text:
(To § 76 para. 1 of the Act)

Equipment whose technical roadworthiness is approved, and requirements for its design and condition, are listed in Part A of Annex No. 12 to this Decree.

p33.heading: Technical requirements for the design and condition of a special vehicle
p33.text:
(To § 78 para. 1 of the Act)

(1) For determining the technical requirements for the design and condition of special vehicles of categories T4.1, T4.2, C, R and S, if the manufacturer requests approval of a non-harmonised type, and for determining the technical requirements for the design and condition of special vehicles of category Z not listed in Annex No. 13 to this Decree, the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the approval of agricultural and forestry vehicles and market surveillance^13) shall apply mutatis mutandis.

(2) Technical requirements for the design and condition of special vehicles of subcategories SS and SN and certain special vehicles of category Z are set out in Annex No. 13 to this Decree.

(3) The model technical certificate for a special vehicle not subject to registration and an extract from this technical certificate are set out in Annex No. 7 to this Decree.

p41.heading: Transitional provision
p41.text: Proceedings initiated before the effective date of this Decree shall be completed in accordance with Decree No. 341/2002 Sb., as effective before the effective date of this Decree.

p42.heading: Repealing provision
p42.text:
The following are hereby repealed:

p43.text:

sprilohac2kvyhlas.text:
Categories of road and special vehicles

- 1. CATEGORY L: the description of category L road vehicles and their further breakdown are set out in the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the approval and market surveillance of two- or three-wheel vehicles and quadricycles^12).

Road vehicles of subcategory LZ are two- or three-wheel vehicles and quadricycles that structurally correspond to road vehicles pursuant to the first sentence and are designed and manufactured for use by armed forces, civil protection, fire services, public order maintenance forces, and emergency medical services.

- 2. CATEGORY M: motor vehicles designed and manufactured primarily for the carriage of persons and their luggage.

- 2.1. Category M1: vehicles of category M with not more than eight seating positions in addition to the driver's seating position. Vehicles belonging to category M1 must not have space for standing passengers. The number of seating positions may be limited to one (i.e., the driver's seating position).

- 2.2. Category M2: vehicles of category M with more than eight seating positions in addition to the driver's seating position and with a maximum mass not exceeding 5 tonnes. Vehicles belonging to category M2 may have, in addition to seating positions, space for standing passengers.

- 2.3. Category M3: vehicles of category M with more than eight seating positions in addition to the driver's seating position and with a maximum mass exceeding 5 tonnes. Vehicles belonging to category M3 may have space for standing passengers.

- 3. CATEGORY N: motor vehicles designed and manufactured primarily for the carriage of goods.

- 3.1. Category N1: vehicles of category N with a maximum mass not exceeding 3.5 tonnes.

- 3.2. Category N2: vehicles of category N with a maximum mass exceeding 3.5 tonnes but not exceeding 12 tonnes.

- 3.3. Category N3: vehicles of category N with a maximum mass exceeding 12 tonnes.

- 4. CATEGORY O: trailers designed and manufactured for the carriage of goods or persons, as well as for the accommodation of persons.

- 4.1. Category O1: vehicles of category O with a maximum mass not exceeding 0.75 tonnes.

- 4.2. Category O2: vehicles of category O with a maximum mass exceeding 0.75 tonnes but not exceeding 3.5 tonnes.

- 4.3. Category O3: vehicles of category O with a maximum mass exceeding 3.5 tonnes but not exceeding 10 tonnes.

- 4.4. Category O4: vehicles of category O with a maximum mass exceeding 10 tonnes.

Vehicle subcategories

Off-road vehicles

An „off-road vehicle“ means a vehicle classified in category M or N that has special technical characteristics enabling its use off normal roads. For these vehicle categories, the letter “G” shall be affixed to the letter and numeral designating the vehicle category. The criteria for classifying a vehicle into the “off-road vehicle” subcategory are set out

in Section 4 of Part A of Annex II to Directive 2007/46/EC.

Special purpose vehicles

A „special purpose vehicle“ means a road vehicle of category M, N or O that has special technical characteristics for performing a function requiring special arrangement and/or equipment. In the case of incomplete vehicles intended to fall into the special purpose vehicle subcategory, the letter “S” shall be affixed to the letter and numeral designating the vehicle category. The different types of special purpose vehicles are defined and listed in Section 5 of Part A of Annex II to Directive 2007/46/EC.

Off-road special purpose vehicle

An „off-road special purpose vehicle“ means a vehicle classified in category M or N that has the special technical characteristics specified in points 2.1 and 2.2 of Part A of Annex II to Directive 2007/46/EC. For these vehicle categories, the letter “G” shall be affixed to the letter and numeral designating the vehicle category. In the case of incomplete vehicles intended to fall into the special purpose vehicle subcategory, the letter “S” shall additionally be affixed as a second suffix to the letter and numeral designating the vehicle category.

- 5. CATEGORY T, C, R and S: the description of category T, C, R and S road vehicles and their further breakdown are set out in the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the approval and market surveillance of agricultural and forestry vehicles^13).

Special vehicles of subcategory SS are self-propelled work machines with their own power source, designed and equipped solely for performing specific work activities. Self-propelled work machines are generally not intended for transport activities.

Special vehicles of subcategory SN are mounted work machines intended solely for performing specific work activities. These machines move on roads suspended from a vehicle and form a single unit with it. Their running gear (if equipped) is not in contact with the road surface in the transport position. This category also includes

interchangeable superstructures having the character of a mounted work machine.

- 6. CATEGORY Z: other vehicles that cannot be classified into any of the above categories.

sprilohac3kvyhlas.text:
Technical requirements for the construction of vehicles of categories M, N, O

- 1. This Annex shall also apply to all vehicle systems, separate technical units and components mounted on or intended for mounting on the aforementioned vehicles.

- 2. Table of applicable European Union directives:

| Item | European Union Directive | Harmonised technical requirements, scope and method of verification of compliance with harmonised technical requirements | Scope and content of documentation containing technical data on the type | Model type-approval certificate | Type-approval mark |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Sound level and exhaust system | 70/157/EEC | Annex I, II and III | Appendix 1 to Annex I and II | Appendix 2 to Annex I and II | Annex II |
| Safety belts and child restraint systems | 91/671/EEC | Article 2 | — | — | — |
| Installation and use of speed limitation devices | 92/06/EEC | Articles 2 to 5 | — | — | — |
| End-of-life vehicles | 2000/53/EC | Annex II | — | — | — |
| Reusability, recyclability and recoverability | 2005/64/EC | Article 6 and Annex I, IV, V | Annex II | Annex III | — |
| Emissions from air conditioning systems | 2006/40/EC | Article 6 and Annex | — | — | — |
| Retrofitting of mirrors | 2007/38/EC | Article 3 | — | — | — |
| Framework for approval | 2007/46/EC | Annex II, IV, IX | Annex III | Annex VI | Article 19, Appendix to Annex VII |

- 3. If systems, separate technical units or components are present on the vehicle, or if such systems, separate technical units or components are subsequently mounted on the vehicle, they must themselves be approved in accordance with the relevant European Union regulations or international agreements in the field of technical fitness approval by which the Czech Republic is bound. This provision does not apply to systems, separate technical units or components that were approved simultaneously with the type-approval of the vehicle as a whole during a single type-approval procedure or a combined type-approval procedure.

- 4. The vehicle must be designed to meet the requirements for right-hand traffic. If the vehicle was originally intended by the manufacturer for left-hand traffic, a technical report from a technical testing laboratory confirming compliance with the requirements for right-hand traffic must be submitted with the vehicle. The report must primarily include an assessment of vehicle lighting, indirect vision, etc.

- 5. For N category vehicles with an undivided box-type body, which have been approved such that there is a partition behind the last row of seats separating the passenger compartment from the cargo area, this partition is not required if the vehicle's design is based on an M1 category passenger car.

- 6. Anti-skid chains

For vehicles of categories M and N, it must be possible to mount anti-skid chains on the wheels of the drive axle or axles, in accordance with the vehicle manufacturer's instructions. In the event of using anti-skid chains in a twin wheel assembly, the design of the vehicle and the chain must ensure that the outer tires in the twin wheel assembly are not overloaded during use. The space between the wheels and the wheel arches, bodywork, vehicle frame, etc., must be large enough to provide sufficient clearance between the fixed parts of the vehicle and the wheels with anti-skid chains at both the operating and maximum technically permissible mass of the vehicle.

- 7. For vehicles where a tachograph record is kept, the tachograph must be verified at least once every 2 years by an authorized metrological center registered by the Ministry and must be sealed, including its drive.

- 8. A coupling device of class A50-X mounted on the vehicle must not obscure the location for the rear registration plate or impair the legibility of the rear registration plate or obscure the prescribed vehicle lighting. If this is not the case, the part with the coupling ball must be removable without the use of tools, or with tools mandatorily located in the vehicle. A vehicle equipped with a towing device approved with a detachable coupling ball without the use of tools must have this ball removed if it is not being used for connection to a trailer or other device.

- 9. The axis of the outlet of the end part of the exhaust pipe, with the exception of pipes directed upwards and exiting above the vehicle, must either be parallel to the horizontal plane or inclined towards the road surface.

- 10. The external surface of vehicles must not have pointed or sharp protrusions directed outwards that would, by their shape, dimensions or hardness, increase the risk of injury to persons.

- 11. For vehicles with a separate box-type body, the rear and side doors, when fully open, must not extend beyond the width of the vehicle by more than their own thickness, and must be reliably secured in this open position.

- 12. Every vehicle must be adequately sprung, with the possible use of shock absorbers and stabilizers.
