Position No. 1 – “M” if it is a small series approval; otherwise, this position is omitted.

Positions No. 2 to 5 – Arabic numerals assigned by the Ministry in sequential order, unique for a specific type.

- 2. Type approval mark for systems, components and separate technical units for non-harmonised approval:

X-C-XXXX

Position No. 1 – “M” if it is a small series approval; otherwise, this position is omitted.

Position No. 2 – “C”.

Positions No. 3 to 6 – Arabic numerals assigned by the Ministry

sprilohac9kvyhlas.text:
Method and form of submitting data to the register of technical data of road vehicle types

- 1. For vehicles with a harmonised type approval certificate, the manufacturer or accredited representative, or the person placing the vehicles on the market (hereinafter referred to as the „submitting person“), shall ensure the electronic submission of data to the Ministry's database to the extent specified in § 4 (3) of the Act, supplemented by the declaration of conformity.

- 2. The Ministry shall, based on the notification of the submitting person, assign a basic number for each new vehicle type of the given make. If there are multiple submitting persons, a different basic number shall be assigned to each of them.

- 3. The submitting person shall ensure the transfer of data from the declaration of conformity for each new, as yet unused combination of type, variant, and version, together with the harmonised type approval number. Each such transfer shall be marked with the assigned basic number and sent to the Ministry's database. The submitting person shall also ensure the transfer of data for the same variant and version of the vehicle if at least one piece of data for that variant and version has changed, for example, due to an extension of the harmonised type approval.

- 4. The printing of the technical certificate is based on the original data. Details of data submission to the Ministry's database are published in the Transport Bulletin.

sprilohac10kvyhla.text:
Requirements for individually manufactured vehicles

- 1. Individually manufactured vehicles must adequately meet the technical requirements, the requirements of relevant European Union regulations, and international agreements in the field of technical fitness approval by which the Czech Republic is bound.

- 2. When applying point 1 of this Annex, no destructive tests are required.

- 3. The model of the individual vehicle approval certificate valid in all Member States is set out in Annex No. 7 to this Decree.

- 4. An integral part of the individual approval certificate is a table of compliance with regulations applicable to EC approval of the given vehicle category and a technical report issued by the testing station.

- 5. Model of the header of the table of compliance with regulations:

| Předmět / subject | Technický předpis – původní znění / technical regulation – original version | Technický předpis – poslední znění / technical regulation – last amendment | Plnění / fulfilling | Poznámka / remark |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  |  |  |  |  |

- 6. In the case of an individually manufactured road vehicle, the municipal authority of a municipality with extended powers shall assign the vehicle identification number. If the manufacturer has an assigned world manufacturer identifier, the municipal authority of a municipality with extended powers shall not assign an official vehicle identification number, but shall use the vehicle identification number assigned to the vehicle by the manufacturer, which corresponds to the assigned world manufacturer identifier.

- 7. Within the individual approval of the technical fitness of a trailer of category O, the maximum design speed of the trailer may be set at no more than 80 km.h^-1. A higher maximum design speed of the vehicle may be set only on the basis of a report issued by a technical testing station, which includes an evaluation of the vehicle's driving characteristics and verification of stability, but not exceeding 130 km.h^-1 for a vehicle of category O1, 100 km.h^-1 for a vehicle of category O2, or 90 km.h^-1 for a vehicle of category O3 or O4.

- 8. Trailers cannot be approved for passenger transport.

sprilohac11kvyhla.text:
Conditions for the conversion of vehicles powered by liquefied petroleum gas or compressed or liquefied natural gas or hydrogen

- 1. Vehicles converted to run on liquefied petroleum gas (hereinafter “LPG”), compressed natural gas (hereinafter “CNG”), or liquefied natural gas (hereinafter “LNG”) must meet the following conditions:

- a) in the event of gas leakage and a malfunction of the gas equipment, this equipment must allow for the immediate closing of the shut-off valves of the pressure vessels, if this is not ensured automatically,

- b) it must be fitted with a sticker placed on the rear of the vehicle in the upper or lower right corner with the inscription LPG, CNG or LNG; this applies to vehicles powered by LPG, CNG or LNG, as well as vehicles with built-in gas equipment used for purposes other than propulsion

- c) the vehicle manufacturer or the manufacturer of gas equipment components or their representative shall determine the maintenance conditions, including the dates and method of performing periodic tests of pressure vessels,

- d) pressure vessels on the vehicle must be placed so that they are not exposed to external heat sources,

- e) a vehicle converted to run on CNG and LNG must comply with ECE Regulation No. 110 and the conditions of this decree; a vehicle converted to run on LPG must comply with ECE Regulation No. 67 and the conditions of this decree; for individual approval of the conversion of a single vehicle, the procedure shall apply mutatis mutandis,

- f) pollutant emissions in exhaust gases must not exceed the specified values for the given category and design of the vehicle, and

- g) the tank manufacturer shall determine its service life; usually, the service life of an LPG tank is 10 years and a CNG tank or cylinder is 20 years.

- 2. Vehicles converted to run on LPG, CNG or LNG must also be equipped by the vehicle manufacturer or the manufacturer of gas equipment components or their representative with an operating manual containing:

- a) safety instructions, information on service life and any regular inspections of tanks, and a description of the procedure in the event of a traffic accident,

- b) information that smoking and handling open flames are prohibited in the vehicle cabin when filling pressure vessels, servicing and maintaining the vehicle; for vehicles equipped with independent heating, this must be out of operation,

- c) information that the contents of gas cylinders may only be discharged into an open space where there is no risk of ignition of the discharged gas, or into containers designated for this purpose,

- d) information that if a gas equipment malfunction occurs, this equipment must be immediately taken out of service; this malfunction also includes the fact that a periodic test of the pressure vessels has not been performed or the service life of the pressure vessel has been exceeded or an approved part of the system has been replaced with an unapproved one, and

- e) information that if a defect specified in paragraph 3 occurs during vehicle operation, the vehicle must be immediately pulled over and safety measures taken.

- 3. A defect in the equipment, upon which the vehicle must be immediately pulled over and safety measures taken, is considered to be:

- a) gas leakage from any part of the gas equipment and malfunction of the ventilation system,

- b) continuous gas discharge by safety valves,

- c) a crack or damage that could cause gas leakage,

- d) malfunction of the reduction device, pressure regulator, mixer or injection valves, pressure gauge, shut-off or check valves and fastening of containers, malfunction of the low-pressure and high-pressure gas supply system,

- e) if gas flows into the mixer or injection valves when the engine is switched off, or

- f) exceeding the permissible limits of pollutants in the exhaust gases.

- 4. For vehicles powered by H2, the requirements set out in paragraphs 1 to 3 shall apply mutatis mutandis. A vehicle converted to run on H2 must comply with ECE Regulation No. 134 and the conditions of this decree. The service life of an H2 tank is usually 15 years.

sprilohac12kvyhla.text:
Technical requirements for the design and condition of equipment

- A) Equipment that must have approved technical capability:

- 1. Antennas for motor vehicles

- 2. Vehicle audio systems

- a) car radios, radio players and their accessories

- b) television and navigation systems intended for installation in the driver's workplace area

- 3. Car mats

- 4. Wheel discs

- a) of light alloys

- b) steel

- 5. Vehicle electrical installations

- a) plugs and sockets for connecting the electrical installations of the towing and towed vehicle, e.g. 7-pin, 13-pin, 15-pin, - 12 V, 24 V)

- b) electrical installations of trailers

- 6. Electrical and electronic equipment of vehicles

- a) windscreen wiper interval switches

- b) electronic logbooks

- c) on-board computers

- d) taximeters

- e) cruise control systems

- f) vehicle telephones and accessories

- g) vehicle fans

- h) vehicle transceivers and accessories

- i) acoustic warning devices signalling reversing

- j) information and ticketing systems

- 7. Luggage carriers

- a) roof

- b) other than roof

- c) bicycle carriers

- d) ski carriers

- e) roof boxes

- 8. Vehicle protection devices

- a) protective frames of vehicles

- b) lateral protection

- c) rear underrun protection

- d) protective covers for the engine and other parts of the vehicle

- 9. External plastic and rubber accessories of vehicles

- 10. Vehicle Tyres

- a) appurtenances:

- b) valves

- c) hull

- d) means for non-dismantling repair of a damaged tyre

- 11. Vehicle seat covers and steering wheel covers

- a) steering wheel covers

- b) seat covers

- c) for seats with airbags

- d) for seats without airbags

- 12. Additional equipment for vehicle engines

- a) devices for adjusting the parameters of engines, automatic transmissions and driving dynamics systems, e.g. ABS, ASR, EDS,

- b) exhaust gas after-treatment system,

- 13. Stickers on vehicle windows

- 14. Snow chains for vehicles

- 15. Coupling devices of vehicles

- 16. Lighting equipment of vehicles, supplementary lighting markings of certain types of vehicles

- 17. Tow ropes, tow bars and lashing strap assemblies

- 18. Vehicle Security Devices

- a) mechanical

- b) electronic

- c) safety marking of glass with a code

- 19. Vehicle control devices

- a) manual controls for persons with physical disabilities

- b) a second control for the needs of driving schools

- 20. Facilities for the transport of persons with reduced mobility

- 21. Glazing of vehicles

- a) roof windows

- b) plastic windows

- c) glass repair technology

- d) tinting of glass

- e) foils - safety, darkening, anti-sun strips

- 22. Branded vehicle accessories

Requirements for Annexes to the Application for Approval of Technical Competence of Vehicle Equipment

- 23. Excerpt from the Commercial Register or similar document.

- 24. Authorization of a foreign manufacturer to represent it in the Czech Republic. The representative of a foreign manufacturer must attach to the application the original or a certified copy of the authorization of the foreign manufacturer to represent it in the Czech Republic and to negotiate matters related to the approval of the technical roadworthiness of equipment in the Czech Republic, in the Czech language. If this document is not drawn up in the Czech language, the applicant shall also submit an authorized translation of the document.

- 25. Technical documentation, which must include:

- a) brief technical description of the product, purpose, function, materials, method and diagram of installation, mounting, into a vehicle, use, basic parameters, etc.,

- b) drawing or assembly drawing with a parts list - e.g., bill of materials for the main structural parts and with the main dimensions of the approved product. The drawing must specify the placement of marks that must be obligatorily indicated on each piece of equipment or product,

- c) other technical documentation, depending on the nature of the equipment being approved,

1) wiring diagram for connection to the vehicle's on-board network,

2) diagram of internal electrical wiring and

3) detailed drawings of significant structural components and

- d) a written statement of the vehicle manufacturer or their representative in the event that the equipment, by its function, design or installation, significantly affects the properties, functions, design or granted international approval of the vehicle for which it is intended. The technical documentation may be supplemented by a catalogue of vehicle equipment if it contains all necessary data. In such a case, the application must state the catalogue number of the equipment, or if there are multiple products of the same group, e.g. radio receivers, electrical accessories, etc., a list of them with catalogue numbers, or national approval numbers, e.g. ABE, must be attached to the application. The technical testing station may request essential technical documentation, especially its descriptive part, in the Czech language.

- 26. Commercial documentation in the Czech language must contain:

- a) operating instructions, which must include, among other things,

1) basic technical data of the product and

2) declaration of conformity of the product in the wording

“This product is identical with the type approved by the Ministry of Transport under approval number.“

- b) assembly instructions

The installation instructions do not have to be incorporated into the commercial documentation if the equipment requires more complex installation and intervention in the vehicle, and is therefore provided by a specialized installation workshop, but is handed over as a separate appendix intended only for authorized installation workshops listed in the list of installation workshops.

The assembly instructions do not need to be issued if the equipment is intended for assembly exclusively by a specialized assembly facility and

- c) maintenance instructions

Maintenance instructions shall be issued only for equipment that requires maintenance during use. If the technical and commercial documentation consists of multiple sheets or pages, these must be numbered.

- 27. List of assembly or service workplaces.

If the equipment does not require maintenance, the applicant must state this fact in the documentation. If the approved equipment by its nature requires professional installation or service, the operating instructions shall include a list of authorized professional workplaces that provide it. In this case, the applicant is responsible for the proper training of the staff of these workplaces. The applicant is always responsible to the approval authority for the correctness and quality of the installation or service. For approval purposes, the list shall be submitted as a separate document. The applicant is obliged to regularly, at least once a year, update the list of installation or service workplaces and its gradual expansion in writing to the Ministry.

- 28. Product sample

The applicant is obliged to submit the necessary number of products to the relevant technical testing laboratory, on which the test will be performed, unless the representative of the technical testing laboratory and the applicant agree otherwise. In the event that it is necessary to test the functionality or assembly of the product on a vehicle, the applicant shall provide this vehicle to the technical testing laboratory, unless they agree otherwise. The applicant is obliged to ensure the possible installation of the approved equipment into the vehicle at their own expense.

- 29. Documents on performed tests

If the applicant has already had some of the partial tests on the submitted product carried out in a foreign technical testing laboratory for national approval in the country of origin or sale, they shall submit the relevant reports on these tests with the application. Test reports carried out in a foreign technical testing laboratory for national approval may be recognised by the technical testing laboratory if the legal regulation in the country of origin of the test is identical to the legal regulation in the Czech Republic. An assessment shall always be carried out for these reports.

- 30. Mandatory marking and design of the production label

For the purpose of approval, the applicant shall submit a proposal for the mandatory marking and the manufacturer's plate.

- a) Each item of approved equipment must be marked in a visible place, without the need to dismantle the product, with the marks described below:

1) manufacturer's mark - logo, inscription, etc. and type of product - trade name, manufacturer's catalog number, etc.,

2) depending on the type of products, further marking may be necessary in some cases, e.g. the mark of the Czech Telecommunication Office, load capacity in kg, electrical voltage, etc.

3) approval number in the following form:

| APPROVAL 8SD |  | XXXX |
| --- | --- | --- |
| designation for approved equipment |  | approval number |

4) if the equipment is also a structural part pursuant to § 2 para 6 of the Act, the approval number shall be in the format:

| CZ XXXX |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| designation for approved component |  | approval number |

The applicant is responsible for ensuring that the above-described mark is visibly placed on each product. This mark must be placed on the product in such a way that it is legible and firmly affixed. The method of affixing the mark is not prescribed. Where this is not possible, the approval number may be placed on the packaging or on a card inserted into the packaging and in the operating instructions.

- b) if the product is provided with a national approval mark that is irremovably affixed to the product or directly made on the product, the applicant shall notify the technical testing station of this fact.

- 31. Changes in the execution of approved equipment

- a) any changes to the approved equipment, e.g. changes in design, marking, name, must be notified in writing by the applicant to the Ministry before their introduction to the market, with a request for approval or implementation of the change. The Ministry shall decide whether new tests and approval will be necessary and, if so, to what extent,

- b) any changes in the name, telephone number or registered office of the applicant must also be notified by the applicant in writing to the Ministry without undue delay, and

- c) if the obligations specified in the preceding paragraphs are not notified without serious reason, the Ministry may suspend the validity of the Certificate of Approval of Technical Competence of Equipment or revoke its validity.

Technical requirements for equipment

- 32. The technical eligibility of a vehicle type shall be approved if the vehicle complies with the technical requirements stipulated in Annexes No. 3 to 6 and 13.

- B) Mandatory equipment of vehicles

Spare wheel and accessories necessary for its replacement

- 1. A „spare wheel and accessories necessary for its replacement“ means

- a) a spare wheel, i.e. a rim with a tyre, which is sufficiently secured in a holder ensuring that the force when removing the wheel from the holder or inserting it into the holder does not exceed 490 N; in the event that multiple wheel sizes are used on the vehicle, the spare wheel must be usable for all these sizes or the vehicle must be equipped with spare wheels for all sizes,

- b) a wrench for vehicle wheel nuts or bolts, and

- c) a hand jack with a load capacity at least equal to the maximum technically permissible axle load of the most heavily loaded axle of the vehicle or combination of vehicles, or equal to the weight of the lifted part of the vehicle from the maximum technically permissible mass of the vehicle when lifting this part in the manner specified by the manufacturer for the use of the jack.

- 2. A spare wheel and accessories necessary for its replacement must be fitted to vehicles of category M, N, O or R, with the exception of

- a) a vehicle in which all wheels are fitted with special construction tyres enabling emergency run-flat operation after a puncture, with an indication of a puncture in any of the tyres,

- b) a vehicle equipped with means for non-dismantling repair of a damaged tire enabling emergency travel,

- c) vehicles with an appropriate contractual relationship on the basis of which the repair of a damaged tire will be ensured continuously throughout the entire territory of the Czech Republic,

- d) a city bus, emergency fire engine, municipal vehicle operated in a limited area within the operational reach of its operator's service facilities, a trailer of category O1 or R1, a fire trailer or a trailer of category R operated in a limited area within the operational reach of its operator's service facilities, or

- e) vehicles in a road train, if another vehicle in that road train is equipped with a spare wheel of the same size and design and with the accessories necessary for its replacement.

First Aid Kit

- 1. The first aid kit must contain

- a) in the case of a motorcycle first aid kit

| Medical supplies | Quantity (pcs) |
| --- | --- |
| Ready-made dressing with 1 pad (width at least 8 cm, absorbency at least 800g/m2) | 1 |
| Ready-made dressing with 2 pads (width at least 8 cm, absorbency at least 800g/m2) | 1 |
| Rubber tourniquet (60 x 1 250 mm) | 1 |
| Rubber (latex) surgical gloves in packaging | 1 |

or

- b) in the case of a car first aid kit

| Medical supplies | quantity (pcs) | | |
| | First aid kit size | | |
| I. | II. | III. | |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Ready-made bandage with 1 pad (width at least 8 cm, absorbency at least 800g/m2) | 3 | 5 | 10 |
| Ready-made bandage with 2 pads (width at least 8 cm, absorbency at least 800g/m2) | 3 | 5 | 10 |
| Smooth adhesive tape on spool (size 2.5 cm x 5 m, min. adhesiveness 7 N/25 mm) | 1 | 2 | 4 |
| Rubber tourniquet bandage (60 x 1,250 mm) | 1 | 3 | 5 |
| Rubber (latex) surgical gloves in packaging | 1 | 2 | 4 |
| Curved scissors (angled) with anti-corrosion treatment and rounded tips - length at least 14 cm | 1 | 1 | 1 |
| Isothermal foil (min. dimensions 200 x 140 cm) | 1 | 1 | 1 |

- 2. A motor vehicle of category L, with the exception of a moped or power-assisted bicycle, must be equipped with a motorcycle first aid kit.

- 3. A motor vehicle, with the exception of a category L vehicle, a single-axle tractor with a trailer or a motor trolley, must be equipped with a „first aid kit“, namely

- a) a vehicle for mass transport of persons with a capacity of more than 80 passengers in size III or with two car first aid kits in size II; for a public transport vehicle, one car first aid kit in size II is sufficient,

- b) a vehicle for mass transport of persons with a capacity of 9 to 80 passengers inclusive, of size II, and

- c) other motor vehicle with at least four wheels of size I.

- 4. A fire engine or an ambulance must be equipped with the appropriate type of first aid kit stipulated by other legal regulations.

- 5. The contents of the first-aid kit shall be stored in a separate case. The components of the first-aid kit must be intact, visually in order, and must not show signs of contamination or other deterioration. The first-aid kit shall be stored in the vehicle in such a space that direct sunlight cannot fall on it. The storage space for the first-aid kit must be dry and clean and must be easily accessible. In a vehicle for mass transport of passengers, the first-aid kit shall be placed in a marked and accessible location in the part of the vehicle designated for passengers.

Warning triangle

A motor vehicle, with the exception of a category L or Z vehicle, a single-axle tractor with a trailer, and a vehicle with an overall width of less than 1 m, must be equipped with a warning triangle approved according to ECE Regulation No. 27.

Fire extinguisher

- 1. A fire extinguisher means a fire extinguisher with adequate extinguishing capability according to the relevant technical regulation^5). The fire extinguisher must have undergone product conformity assessment by an authorized body^7).

- 2. A fire extinguisher must be carried in a vehicle of category M2, M3 or an ambulance, namely

- a) a vehicle of category M2 or M3, with a maximum of 22 seats for passengers in addition to the driver's seat, with one or more fire extinguishers with a minimum extinguishing capacity of 21 A or 113 B,

- b) another vehicle of category M2 or M3 with one or more fire extinguishers with a minimum extinguishing capacity of 43 A or 183 B or a fire extinguisher according to ECE Regulation No. 107 or an automatic fire suppression system in combination with at least one fire extinguisher with an extinguishing capacity of 21 A or 113 B placed near the driver's seat, and

- c) an ambulance with a fire extinguisher with a fire extinguishing capacity of 34 B.

- 3. The fire extinguisher shall be secured in the vehicle in a vertical or horizontal holder so that it reliably withstands an acceleration of at least 6 g in the direction of a frontal impact of the vehicle, and shall be placed in a clearly visible and easily accessible location, with one extinguisher installed in the immediate vicinity of the vehicle driver.

- C) Technical requirements for the equipment of bicycles, animal-drawn vehicles, and handcarts:

- 1. Bicycles must be equipped

- a) two independent effective brakes with graduated control of the braking effect; bicycles for preschool children equipped with a freewheel hub with a back-pedal brake do not have to be equipped with a front brake,

- b) the open ends of the handlebar tube must be reliably capped, e.g., with plugs, grips, etc.,

- c) the ends of brake control levers and the free ends of handlebars must have edges either covered with energy-absorbing material, or if rigid materials are used, the edges must have a radius of curvature of at least 3.2 mm; gear shift levers, wing nuts, quick-release wheel hub skewers, mudguard brackets and ends must have edges either covered with energy-absorbing material, or if rigid materials are used, the edges must have a radius of at least 3.2 mm in one plane and at least 2 mm in the other plane perpendicular to it,

- d) wheel hub nuts, unless they are wing nuts, quick-release nuts or combined with an end cap, must be closed,

- e) a red rear reflector; this reflector may be combined with a red rear lamp or replaced by reflective materials of similar properties; the area of the reflector must not be less than 2000 mm^2, while the inscribed quadrilateral must have one side at least 40 mm long; the reflector must be firmly placed in the longitudinal median plane of the bicycle or on the left side as close as possible to it at a height of 250 - 900 mm above the road surface; the active surface of the reflector must be perpendicular to the road surface with a tolerance of +/- 15 deg. and perpendicular to the longitudinal median plane of the bicycle with a tolerance of +/- 5 deg.; reflective materials replacing the rear reflector may also be placed on the cyclist's clothing or footwear,

- f) a white front reflector; this reflector may be replaced by reflective materials of similar properties; the reflector must be placed in the longitudinal median plane above the surface of the front wheel tyre when the bicycle is stationary; the area of the reflector must not be less than 2000 mm2, with the inscribed quadrilateral having one side at least 40 mm long; the active surface of the reflector must be perpendicular to the road plane with a tolerance of +/- 15 degrees and perpendicular to the longitudinal median plane of the bicycle with a tolerance of +/- 5 degrees; reflective materials replacing the reflector may also be placed on the cyclist's clothing or footwear,

- g) by orange reflectors (auto-yellow) be fitted on both sides of the treads - pedals; these reflectors may be replaced by retro-reflective materials placed on or near the footwear, and

- h) on the spokes of the front or rear wheel or both wheels, at least one amber (automotive yellow) side reflector on each side of the wheel; the area of the reflector must not be less than 2000 mm^2, with the inscribed quadrilateral having one side at least 20 mm long; these reflectors may be replaced by reflective materials on the sides of the wheel or on the sides of the tire casings or on the ends of the mudguards or on the side parts of the cyclist's clothing.

- 2. Bicycles for riding in reduced visibility must also be equipped with the following light signaling and lighting devices:

- a) a headlamp emitting white light forward; the headlamp must be permanently adjusted and set so that the reference axis of the luminous flux intersects the road surface plane at a distance of no more than 20 m from the headlamp and so that this adjustment cannot change spontaneously or due to unintentional intervention by the cyclist; if the road is sufficiently and continuously illuminated, the headlamp may be replaced by a lamp emitting white intermittent light,

- b) a rear lamp emitting red light; the conditions for the placement of this lamp are identical to the conditions for the placement and fastening of the rear reflector according to paragraph 1 letter e); the rear lamp emitting red light may be combined with a red rear reflector according to paragraph 1 letter e); the rear lamp emitting red light may be replaced by a flashing red light and

- c) if the electric power source is an energy storage source, its capacity must ensure the luminosity of the lights according to letters a) and b) for at least 1.5 hours without interruption.

- 3. The lighting equipment of a bicycle is not considered equipment within the meaning of Section 32 of Act No. 361/2000 Sb.

- 4. If the bicycle is equipped with an auxiliary seat for transporting a child, this seat must be firmly attached and fitted with firm footrests for the child. The seat and footrests must be designed and positioned so that the child cannot be injured during the ride and the safety of the ride is not jeopardized. If the bicycle is equipped with a luggage carrier, this carrier must be properly and reliably attached and must not affect the safety of the ride.

- 5. Tyres and rims must not show cracks, fissures or other obvious deformations that would clearly impair driving safety.

- 6. Bicycles placed on the market must have a clearly legible serial number durably marked on an easily accessible part of the frame or be equipped with a device reliably replacing it. In this case, a device reliably replacing the serial number is also considered to be, for example, an electronic carrier of such information, which will be firmly attached to the bicycle frame.

- 7. Bicycles placed on the market, if not equipped in accordance with point 2 of this Annex, i.e. for riding in reduced visibility, must be provided with a clear and unambiguous warning in the operating instructions that these bicycles are not, given their current equipment, suitable for operation on roads in reduced visibility.

- 8. A bicycle may be additionally equipped with an auxiliary motor if

- a) the original character of the bicycle according to points 1 and 2 will be preserved,

- b) the auxiliary motor shall adequately fulfil the conditions of the provisions of § 19 of the Act,

- c) its output does not exceed 1 kW,

- d) if an internal combustion engine is used, such engine shall not have a cylinder or cylinders with a displacement greater than 50 cm^3,

- e) the maximum design speed will not exceed 25 km.h^-1 and

- f) the installation of a propulsion system - engine, fuel tank or battery on a bicycle - will not require intervention in its load-bearing parts.

If the vehicle meets all the above requirements, it shall continue to be considered a „bicycle“ for the purposes of this decree.

- 9. For the purposes of this decree, a bicycle shall also mean tricycles and multi-wheelers, as well as multi-seat bicycles - tandems and similar vehicles powered by human force and also intended for operation on roads, such as scooters.

Vehicles so defined, whose width exceeds 1.0 m, must be equipped with dual lighting in accordance with point 1 letters e) and f) and point 2 letters a) and b), which must be placed symmetrically to the longitudinal axis of the vehicle, at a maximum distance of 0.2 m from the longitudinal plane marking the width of the vehicle. The design requirements specified in points 1 to 8 shall apply mutatis mutandis to these vehicles.

For the purposes of this point, width means the distance between two planes parallel to the longitudinal plane of the vehicle and tangential to the vehicle on both sides of this plane. All parts of the vehicle, and especially all fixed parts protruding sideways, must be located between these two planes, with the exception of the rear-view mirror or mirrors.

- 10. For the purposes of this decree, a bicycle shall also mean pedal bicycles equipped with an auxiliary electric motor pursuant to the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the approval and market surveillance of two- or three-wheel vehicles and quadricycles^12).

- 11. A bicycle trailer must comply with the requirements of another legal regulation^9)

- 12. Upholstered vehicles must be equipped with

- a) at least one brake that is easily, quickly and safely operable,

- b) at the front with two white reflectors and at the rear with two red reflectors, identical and identically placed as the reflectors prescribed for trailers, and

- c) in reduced visibility, at the front with a white-light lamp on the side facing the center of the roadway or with two white-light lamps on each side of the vehicle indicating its maximum overall width; at the rear with two red-light lamps on each side of the vehicle indicating its maximum overall width.

- 13. A hand cart whose width exceeds 0.6 m must be equipped with red non-triangular reflectors placed symmetrically as close as possible to the side outlines of the cart at the same height above the road.

- D) Technical requirements for vehicle lighting equipment:

- 1. Vehicles of category M, N, O, L, T, C, R or S may only be equipped with light sources and devices that are stipulated for the given type and category of vehicle based on technical requirements valid at the time of vehicle manufacture or later technical requirements, including requirements for their number and connection. Lighting devices may be used on vehicles if they meet the conditions stipulated by the technical regulations listed for motor vehicles and their trailers in Annex No. 3 to this Decree, for two-wheel and three-wheel vehicles and their trailers in the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the approval and market surveillance of two- or three-wheel vehicles and quadricycles^12), for tractors and their trailers in the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the approval and market surveillance of agricultural and forestry vehicles^13), for mobile machinery in Annex No. 13 to this Decree, and for other special vehicles in Annex No. 6 to this Decree.

- 2. Vehicles must not be equipped with light devices other than those prescribed or permitted in accordance with paragraph 1 or those permitted under paragraphs 3 to 5.

- 3. Vehicles may be equipped with additional lighting devices to mark taxi vehicles, driving school vehicles, public transport vehicles – illuminated directional and numerical signs, ambulance vehicles, fire brigade vehicles and vehicles of the Police of the Czech Republic, municipal police, Customs Administration and Military Police. For ambulance vehicles, the use of a light sign “ambulance” is permitted, shining forward with an uninterrupted white light or red light, which is active exclusively when special blue warning lights are used. For vehicles of the Police of the Czech Republic, municipal police, Customs Administration and Military Police, a forward and backward shining sign is permitted, for example “STOP”, “POLICIE STOP”, “POZOR KOLONA” or “STOP KOLONA”. For fire brigade vehicles, a forward and backward shining sign “POZOR KOLONA” or “STOP KOLONA” is permitted. These signs shining forward may also be mirrored. Installation is only possible provided that they do not emit red light forward and white light backward at an angle of 15 degrees outwards from the vehicle's outline, with the exception of the sign “ambulance” in conjunction with the use of special blue warning lights or signs on vehicles of the Police of the Czech Republic, municipal police, Customs Administration and Military Police. These devices must be placed on the vehicle in such a way that the driver's view is not obstructed, nor is the prescribed geometric visibility of other lighting devices that emit uninterrupted light of unchangeable color and whose installation is approved. The additional installation of reflectors, reflective foils, contour marking of vehicles, etc., is also considered the installation of a lighting device subject to approval. The installation of these devices must meet the requirements of technical regulations. Rear marking plates for heavy and long vehicles must be approved according to ECE Regulation No. 70, and rear marking plates for slow vehicles must be approved according to ECE Regulation No. 69, and retro-reflective contour marking materials must be approved according to ECE Regulation No. 104.

- 4. The provision of paragraph 3 shall not apply to equipping vehicles with lighting devices emitting white light intended for

- a) for illuminating the loading area of the vehicle,

- b) for illuminating the coupling device for trailers,

- c) at the rear of semi-trailer tractors for illuminating semi-trailers when coupling vehicles into combinations,

- d) for illuminating towed implements for work exclusively off public roads,

- e) as a searchlight or

- f) as work lamps,

these devices must not be used in normal road traffic. The operation of these headlamps must be independent of other external lighting devices of the vehicle and must be signalled in the driver's field of vision by a continuously illuminated yellow indicator.

- 5. Vehicles not equipped with their own external lighting system, i.e., work machines, etc., must be equipped for road traffic with a set of portable lighting devices – position, brake, and direction indicator lights – that meet the requirements for the relevant vehicle category. For towed vehicles, the set of lighting devices must be fitted with a connecting cable of appropriate length, with a plug enabling connection to the towing vehicle. The holders for this set must be positioned so that, once the set is installed, the requirements for the placement of lighting devices on the vehicle are met.

- E) Technical requirements for the equipment of vehicles with special warning light and sound devices

- 1. In addition to the prescribed external lighting devices, the following must be equipped with one or more special warning light devices emitting amber light of an approved type or design

- a) motor vehicles and trailers performing work while driving or performing work while the vehicle is stationary on the roadway or shoulder,

- b) motor vehicles and trailers whose dimensions or weight exceed the limits stipulated in another legal regulation, if so stipulated by the Ministry approving the type-approval of the vehicle or by the municipal authority of a municipality with extended powers in the case of individual vehicle approval,

- c) motor vehicles and trailers which, together with their load, exceed the limit values stipulated in another legal regulation, if so determined by the authority that issued the permit for special use of the road pursuant to another legal regulation,^2)

- d) motor vehicles and trailers for which this is stipulated by the Ministry approving the technical roadworthiness of the vehicle type or by the municipal authority of a municipality with extended powers in the case of approving the technical roadworthiness of a conversion or of an individual vehicle, or

- e) self-propelled and towed work machines whose width exceeds 3.00 m or for which this is stipulated by the Ministry approving the technical eligibility of the vehicle type or by the municipal authority of a municipality with extended powers in the case of approving the technical eligibility of an individual vehicle.

- 2. Tractors may be equipped with a special amber warning light device, which, however, may only be activated if any of the cases listed in paragraph 1 letters a) to e) occur.

- 3. The activation of a special warning light device emitting blue or amber light and the operation of supplementary special warning lights emitting an intermittent flow of blue or amber light are independent of other external lighting devices of the vehicle and are equipped with a yellow indicator controllable from the driver's seat, with the exception of amber lights affixed to the vehicle body by means of a magnet or vacuum suction cup without a separate switch. A special warning device with two levels of luminosity must be wired so that it always reacts as a whole to changes in ambient light intensity, either automatically or via a control located within reach of the driver. The operating manual for the end user must contain information on the use of the special warning device.

- 4. The colour of a special warning light, blue or orange, expresses the special nature of vehicles and their position in relation to all road users.

- 5. The special warning light device shall be located, if possible, at the highest point of the bodywork or superstructure or as close as possible to the highest point, approximately in the longitudinal median plane of the vehicle or approximately symmetrically on both sides of this plane. In the case of a special blue warning light device attached to the vehicle bodywork by means of a magnet or a vacuum suction cup, the placement of this device on one side of the vehicle is permitted.

- 6. Special warning light devices shall be placed on the vehicle in such a way that at least one is always directly visible from any point on a horizontal plane 1 m above the roadway, 20 m away from this light source.

- 7. Special warning light devices emitting blue light may be supplemented on the vehicle by a maximum of one pair of additional special warning lights emitting an intermittent flow of blue light of an approved type, shining forward and placed symmetrically to the longitudinal vertical plane, which are placed with their lowest point of the active luminous surface not lower than 400 mm and their highest point of the active luminous surface not higher than 1500 mm above the road surface. A special warning light device emitting blue light is always supplemented by a special acoustic warning device emitting an acoustic signal with continuously variable pitch - a siren. The frequency of pitch changes may be variable. Vehicles may further be equipped with one pair of additional special warning lights with an intermittent flow of blue light directed backwards, one pair of additional special warning lights with an intermittent flow of blue light directed to the sides placed in the front part of the vehicle, and a third additional special light with an intermittent flow of blue light directed forwards placed behind the front windshield of the vehicle.

- 8. Special warning light devices emitting amber light may be supplemented by a maximum of five additional special warning lamps emitting an intermittent amber light stream of an approved type at the front and five at the rear, placed symmetrically to the longitudinal vertical plane. Their lowest point of the active luminous surface must be placed no lower than 400 mm above the road surface. The lamps must be arranged on the vehicle in a single horizontal row such that the upper edge of the active luminous surface of the lamps may not exceed the upper outline of the vehicle by more than their height. They may be replaced by thirteen additional special warning lamps of an approved type emitting an intermittent amber light stream at the front and thirteen at the rear, placed symmetrically to the longitudinal vertical plane. The lamps must be placed with their lowest point of the active luminous surface no lower than 400 mm above the road surface. The arrangement of the lamps may be in a square with the side and bottom edges having a maximum of four lamps. The square is placed on the vehicle such that the upper edge of the active luminous surface of the upper lamps must not exceed the upper outline of the vehicle. If the lamps are also used on a trailer, their number may be up to 24. A special warning light device emitting amber light cannot be supplemented by a special audible warning device.

- 9. Special warning light devices must comply with the conditions stipulated by ECE Regulation No. 65 and must be approved in accordance therewith. Supplementary warning lights must be approved. Vehicles that may be equipped with a special sound device supplemented by a special blue warning light device are stipulated by another legal regulation.^10)

- 10. Only approved special sound warning devices may be used for installation on a vehicle. It shall be placed on the vehicle in such a way that it is not damaged or its sound properties changed during operation under any conditions. The lower sound level of these devices is set at a minimum of 105 dB(A).

- 11. Vehicles equipped with a special acoustic warning device supplemented by a special blue warning light device may also be equipped with contour or line marking with retro-reflection approved according to ECE Regulation No. 104 for the purpose of visibility of these vehicles with regard to the special nature of their use in road traffic.

- 12. The requirements for special warning light devices emitting blue light shall apply mutatis mutandis to special warning light devices emitting blue and red light. Special warning light devices emitting blue and red light must have a red to blue light ratio of 1:1; this requirement also applies to supplementary special warning lights emitting an intermittent light stream. A combination of special warning light devices emitting blue light with supplementary special warning lights emitting an intermittent stream of blue and red light is also permissible, provided that the ratio of emitted blue and red light in these lights is maintained at 1:1.

- F) Technical requirements for vehicle equipment with wheels, tires, and anti-skid chains:

- 1. The vehicle must be equipped in such a way that the design, construction, dimensions, and inflation of the tires correspond to the operating conditions, especially the maximum permissible vehicle weight, the permissible loads on the axles, and its maximum design speed. The speed category of the tires must be the same as or higher than the maximum design speed of the vehicle.

- 2. If the vehicle is equipped with winter tyres pursuant to paragraph 13 with a lower speed category than the maximum design speed of the vehicle, a warning label must be affixed inside the vehicle in a place always visible from the driver's perspective, indicating the lowest value of the maximum speed category of tyres for driving on snow. The information on the sticker specifies the maximum permissible speed of the vehicle with the winter tyres fitted, which must not be exceeded during vehicle operation. The warning in the form of a sticker may be replaced by a device permanently installed in the vehicle, such as an on-board computer.

- 3. Unless otherwise stipulated upon type-approval, a vehicle may only be fitted with tyres designated for the given vehicle type by the vehicle manufacturer. The load capacity of the tyres must not be lower than the permissible load per wheel or axle of the vehicle. For emergency use, a wheel with a tyre of a different or special design or a different dimension, designated for this purpose by the vehicle manufacturer and the tyre manufacturer, may be used as a spare wheel with a tyre. These wheels must be approved in accordance with ECE Regulation No. 64 or, before 1 January 2014, also in accordance with Directive 92/23/EEC.

- 4. Tyres must always be properly inflated to the pressure prescribed by the vehicle manufacturer. In the case of dual wheel assembly, the valves for inflating the inner tyre and wheel must be arranged in such a way that the air pressure in the tyre can be measured or adjusted from the outer wheel side without dismounting the wheels or other difficult manipulation. In the case of dual wheel assembly, the valves for inflating the inner tyre and wheel must be arranged in such a way that the air pressure in the tyre can be measured or adjusted from the outer wheel side, without dismounting the wheels or other difficult manipulation. Spare tyres must be inflated to at least the pressure corresponding to the highest prescribed tyre inflation on the vehicle.

- 5. Tyre casings must not have cracks or damage on their outer circumference that expose or damage the carcass. The active surface of a tyre casing in operation must have a clearly visible tread around its entire circumference and across its entire width of the top tread, with a minimum depth of the main tread grooves or sipes of 1.0 mm for mopeds and 1.6 mm for vehicles of other categories. Main tread grooves are understood to mean grooves equipped with wear indicators, marked with the abbreviation TWI.

- 6. The mutual difference in the outer diameters of individual unloaded tires on the same axle or in a dual assembly must not be greater than 1.5% of the outer diameter.

- 7. A „retreaded tyre“ is a tyre that has been produced by retreading a tyre by replacing the worn tread with new material. It may also include retreading the outer surface of the sidewalls. Retreaded tyres for vehicles of categories M and N and their trailers must comply with the requirements of ECE Regulation No. 108 or ECE Regulation No. 109 and must be approved thereunder.

- 8. Class II and Class III buses (vehicles of categories M2 and M3) and emergency fire-fighting vehicles must not be equipped with retreaded tyres on the front axle.

- 9. Tyres for vehicles of categories M and N and their trailers may only be repaired professionally, based on recommended standards, e.g. ČSN 63 1910, ČSN 63 1912, NSPP-01-93. For emergency travel, repairs may be carried out using approved preparations applied by injecting a solution into the tyre or by pre-vulcanised repair materials without dismounting the casing. It is impermissible to use an inner tube in an unrepaired tubeless casing. Repaired casings may be used in the same or a lower speed and load capacity category. When changing the speed or load capacity category, the original marking must be removed and replaced with a new permanent marking. If the casing cannot be used as tubeless after repair, the TUBELESS marking on both sidewalls must be removed. The use of inner tubes is permitted in the case of minor punctures or minor damage to the beads with subsequent loss of tubeless casing sealing.

- 10. The tread grooves of tyres designated by the tyre manufacturer for vehicles of categories M2, M3, N and their trailers may be additionally deepened only in the manner prescribed by the tyre manufacturer. On both sides of the tyre sidewall, the symbol Theta - the diameter of the circle must be at least 20 mm or the inscription “REGROOVABLE“ must be marked. Deepening the tread grooves of passenger car tyres is not permitted.

- 11. The vehicle must not be equipped with studded tires, with the exception of emergency service vehicles. This prohibition also applies to vehicles in international traffic.

- 12. Vehicles may be equipped with anti-skid chains or similar devices only of an approved type, which are supplied at the time of sale with assembly instructions and with an indication of the tire dimensions on which they may be mounted.

- 13. A vehicle must not, with the exception of emergency driving, be simultaneously equipped with tyres of different sizes and constructions, unless otherwise stipulated upon approval of technical roadworthiness. Only identical tyres must be used on the same axle. Tyre construction means diagonal, radial, mixed - BIAS BELTED construction. An identical tyre means a tyre of the same size, construction, tread pattern type and brand name. Tyre tread pattern type means summer tread pattern and winter tread pattern, which is marked on the tyre sidewall with M+S, M.S, M/S or MS; a special tread pattern, which is marked on the tyre sidewall with ET, ML, MPT or POR, is also considered a winter tread pattern.

- 14. Tyres marked with the inscription „FRT“ must not be used on steered axles of trailers and motor vehicles and on drive axles.

- G) Technical requirements for vehicle equipment with supplementary devices:

- 1. The free end of an antenna longer than 1.40 m that would extend beyond the plan projection of the vehicle's outline, with the exception of retractable antennas, must be attached to the vehicle in such a way that the antenna does not extend beyond the plan outline of the vehicle. Antennas must, in terms of external projections, comply with the requirements of ECE Regulation No. 26 either independently as components or within the scope of the approval of the entire vehicle according to this regulation.

- 2. Luggage carriers etc., advertising boards, direction signs, taxi vehicle markings, driving school vehicle markings and other equipment with which the vehicle is additionally equipped must be reliably attached to the vehicle, must not extend beyond the vehicle's ground plan outline with the exception of the rear of the vehicle, and must not have any spikes or sharp edges; external parts including fastening parts must have a radius of curvature of at least 2.5 mm. Exceptions to this radius of curvature value are possible according to the provisions of ECE Regulation No. 26. Luggage carriers must allow for reliable fastening of transported items. The fastening of the carrier, including luggage, must reliably withstand deceleration in the longitudinal direction of at least 6 g. Luggage carriers and ski carriers must meet the requirements of ECE Regulation No. 26 in terms of external projections either independently as components or as part of the approval of the entire vehicle according to this regulation.

- 3. Modifications and repairs to vehicle glazing that result in a reduction of light transmittance may only be carried out in compliance with the conditions stipulated by ECE Regulation No. 43 and must be approved according to this annex to the decree. The vehicle in the driver's field of vision must not be equipped with any objects, e.g., decorative and souvenir items, that would restrict the driver's view in all directions, with the exception of approved markings intended for placement on vehicle windows or markings or devices mandatorily placed according to other legal regulations, such as a functional electronic device for toll payment or a valid coupon proving payment of a time-based fee. The provisions of this paragraph do not apply to the rear windows of category M3 vehicles. The view through the rear window of a category M1 vehicle may be partially reduced by approved accessories or transported items, but only on condition that the vehicle is equipped with a right external rearview mirror of an approved type. The view through the front window of category M3 vehicles may be partially reduced, in addition to approved markings intended for placement on windows, only by a destination sign. The design and placement of signs on the vehicle must be approved according to this annex to the decree.

- 4. Reserve fuel containers shall be designed in such a way that fuel cannot leak out in any position of the container. Containers in the vehicle must be placed at a distance of at least 150 mm from the planes defining the maximum width of the vehicle and at least 250 mm from the plane defining the length of the vehicle at the front or rear.

- 5. Bars or ropes intended for towing vehicles must be clearly visible; bars must be marked along their entire length with transverse red and white stripes 75 mm wide, ropes must be marked with a red flag or tag measuring at least 300 x 300 mm. Bars or ropes intended for towing vehicles must be of an approved type. A condition for their approval is their resistance to an axial force of 12 kN.

- 6. Independently mounted additional heaters must be approved as a component in accordance with ECE Regulation No. 122 or, until 1 November 2014, also in accordance with Directive 2001/56/EC. Installation into a vehicle must be carried out by an authorized service facility to which the Ministry has granted such authorization.

- 7. Road motor vehicles with a maximum permissible mass exceeding 3.50 t and trailers with a maximum permissible mass exceeding 750 kg must be equipped with at least one wheel chock. Road motor vehicles and trailers with three or more axles, single-axle and double-axle trailers with a maximum permissible mass exceeding 750 kg, and semi-trailers must be equipped with at least two wheel chocks. The chocks must effectively secure the vehicle against unintentional movement, must be easily accessible to the operator, and safely graspable. The wheel chocks must be secured on the vehicle in such a way that they cannot become unintentionally dislodged during operation.

- 8. Vehicles of categories M2, M3, N2 and N3 must be retrofitted with speed limiters if so stipulated by the European Union regulation governing the installation and use of speed limiters^15).

- 9. Vehicles of categories M1 and N1, class I, must be equipped in terms of emissions from air conditioning systems as stipulated by the European Union regulation governing emissions from motor vehicle air conditioning systems^16).

- 10. N2 and N3 category vehicles must be retrofitted with mirrors if so stipulated by the European Union regulation governing the equipping of heavy goods vehicles with mirrors^17).

- H) Technical requirements for vehicle equipment with maximum permitted speed marking:

- 1. Vehicles of categories M2, M3, N2, N3, O, R, S, C, T and Z, with the exception of front-mounted machines of category SN and single-axle tractors with trailers and special carriers of work attachments, must be equipped on the rear of the body, if the vehicle's design allows it, in the left half, with a rounded indication of the maximum permitted speed

- a) for vehicles with a design speed not exceeding 45 km.h^-1, to the nearest lower integer,

- b) for other vehicles, to the nearest lower whole number divisible by five.

- 2. The maximum permitted speed marking shall be in the form of a white circle with a red border with an outer diameter of 200 mm; letters in the circle, if used, must have a height of „k“ 35 mm, „m“ 24 mm, digits 75 to 80 mm, letter line thickness 6 mm and digit line thickness 12 mm. The colour of the inscription shall be black. The maximum permitted speed marking must always be legible and must not be obscured during operation. The marking is required to be made of retroreflective material class 1 according to ČSN EN 12899-1, i.e. red and white, letters and digits are non-reflective. Retroreflective material is not required for vehicles for which operation in reduced visibility is prohibited, for special vehicles and mounted machinery.

- 3. If it is not possible to place a marking with a diameter of 200 mm on O1, O2, R category trailers, S category vehicles, single-axle tractors with trailers, and special carriers of work attachments, it is permissible to use a marking with an outer diameter of 150 mm; in such case, the letters in the circle, if used, must have a height of „k“ 20 mm, „m“ 14 mm, the digits 75 to 80 mm, the line thickness for letters is 3.5 mm and for digits 12 mm.

- I) Technical requirements for the equipment of special vehicles:

- 1. Special vehicles, when operated on roads, must be equipped in such a way that working tools are transported only in an approved transport position. A special vehicle must be equipped in such a way that it does not cause pollution or damage to the road. Its containers and working tools, e.g. loading bucket, pallet forks, must be empty, i.e. without contents, load, burden, etc. The transport of cargo on a forklift, or telescopic handler with forks, is permitted only for unloading and loading goods.

- 2. A tractor exceeding a width of 2.55 m, e.g., by dual mounting of rear axle wheels or otherwise, must be equipped with warning signs indicating the vehicle's outline and a special amber warning light device, which must be activated; its maximum speed is reduced to 20 km.h^-1. In reduced visibility, the operation of such a tractor, even if so equipped, is not permitted if the mounted working equipment restricts the geometric visibility of the tractor's lighting devices, or if such restriction was stipulated during the approval of the mounted working equipment. Only an attached or mounted machine, not a trailer or semi-trailer, may be coupled to the tractor.

- 3. A tractor with a front-mounted machine or a front-mounted interchangeable implement covering the tractor's headlamps must be equipped with additional dipped-beam headlamps, which must be activated when operating on roads. The additional headlamps must comply with the conditions stipulated by ECE Regulation No. 86 or a directly applicable European Union regulation governing the approval and market surveillance of agricultural and forestry vehicles^13).

- 4. When operated on roads, tracked vehicles must be equipped with rubber tracks or metal tracks with rubber blocks on the support surfaces; if the support rollers are independently sprung and fitted with rubber tires, these must have a minimum height of 40 mm. The rubber blocks or tires must not be harder than 70 Shore. The use of other materials with equivalent properties is also permitted.

- 5. In the event of non-compliance with paragraph 4, snow track vehicles - snow groomers, snowmobiles, may only be used on roadways if the traction bars of the tracks do not touch the road surface, and only if the road surface is covered with a sufficiently thick layer of snow.

- 6. Vehicles which, due to their design, cannot be equipped with their own external lighting device must be equipped with a set of portable lighting devices when in operation.

- 7. For vehicles or vehicle combinations where the distance from the front outline to the center of the steering wheel exceeds 4.00 m, their exit from places located off the road, passage through blind intersections, etc., must be ensured with the assistance of a competent and duly instructed person.

- 8. A tractor in a road train with a work machine must be equipped with rear-view mirrors in such a way as to ensure perfect visibility, if necessary by extending the mirror holders, etc.
