Technical requirements for the construction of category Z vehicles and subcategories SS and SN

Technical requirements for special vehicles of subcategory SS

- 1. Special vehicles of subcategory SS are self-propelled work machines with their own power source, designed and equipped solely for performing specific work activities and are not generally intended for transport activities, e.g., excavators, loaders, dozers, scrapers, graders, rollers, milling machines, finishers, ditch cleaners, cleaning machines, combine harvesters, forage harvesters, forklifts, etc.

- 2. For self-propelled work machines (hereinafter referred to as “machines”), the provisions of this Annex and the provisions of the Decree concerning

- a) vehicle dimensions

- b) marking of vehicle outlines

- c) vehicle weights

- d) wheels and tires

- e) marking of certain data on the vehicle

- f) manufacturer's or factory plate, serial number

- g) special warning light devices

- h) placement of the registration plate and

- i) mandatory equipment

- 3. When operating on roads, the machine must be in the approved transport position. No trailers or interchangeable towed machines may be attached to the machine on roads, with the exception of the machine's working equipment transported on a chassis, unless otherwise stipulated when its technical fitness is approved. The chassis with the working equipment must meet the conditions stipulated for interchangeable towed machines. Overrun braking systems may be fitted to chassis with machine working equipment whose maximum permissible mass does not exceed 8.00 t. The operating mass of the machine must be equal to or greater than the instantaneous mass of the chassis; in terms of braking effects, the vehicle combination must meet the requirements of Article 4 of this Annex. If the machine is equipped with a coupling device, this device must secure the attached chassis against unintentional detachment with a double mechanical safety lock.

- 4. The braking system of machines intended for agricultural and forestry activities with a maximum design speed not exceeding 30 km.h^-1 must comply with the conditions stipulated by technical regulation “ECE document - TRANS/SC 1/WP 29/R.274”. The braking system of machines intended for agricultural and forestry activities with a maximum design speed not exceeding 40 km.h^-1 must comply with the conditions stipulated in the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the approval of agricultural and forestry vehicles and market surveillance^13) or ČSN EN ISO 3450.

- 5. The braking system of machines intended for agricultural and forestry activities with a maximum design speed exceeding 40 km.h^-1 must comply with the conditions for service, emergency, and parking braking stipulated by ECE technical regulation No. 13.

For the calculation of braking distance in type 0 test, the following formulas apply:

service braking

s<=0.15v+v2115

emergency braking

s<=0.15v+2v2/115

The test speed of the vehicle shall be set at v = 50 km.h^-1; for machines that do not reach this speed, v = maximum design speed.

The maximum design speed of the machine is the maximum speed specified by the machine manufacturer. The actual maximum speed of the machine determined by the test must not differ from the maximum design speed by more than +5% under the prescribed conditions.

Type I test shall be performed under the conditions stipulated for category N2 vehicles.

The parking braking system must hold a stationary loaded vehicle on an uphill and downhill gradient of 18%.

During tests, the force applied to the control device required to achieve the prescribed braking effects must not exceed 700 N if the control device is a pedal, and 400 N on a hand-operated control device.

Braking effects are evaluated at both the operating and maximum permissible mass of the machine or vehicle combination and the distribution of masses on individual axles approved for road operation.

Alternatively to the above requirements, the braking system of machines mentioned in this point may comply with the conditions stipulated by technical standard ČSN EN ISO 3450.

- 6. The braking system of machines intended for construction and similar activities must comply with the conditions stipulated by technical standards ČSN EN ISO 3450, ČSN EN 500-4, ČSN ISO 6292.

- 7. The steering must comply with the conditions stipulated in technical standards or in technical regulation ISO 10998, in the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the approval of agricultural and forestry vehicles and market surveillance^13), ČSN EN 12643, and must be approved accordingly.

- 8. Machines with a maximum design speed exceeding 20 km.h^-1 must have all wheels fitted with effective covers, e.g., mudguards and wheel arches.

- 9. Wheel covers must meet the following conditions:

- a) for machines intended for agricultural and forestry activities at the operating mass of the machine, the wheel covers must cover the wheels so that the front edge of the front axle wheels is in front of the vertical plane passing through the center of that wheel, and the rear edge of the front axle wheels is below the horizontal plane passing through the center of that wheel, and both the front and rear edges of the rear axle wheels are at most one quarter of the wheel diameter above the horizontal plane passing through the center of that wheel. Mudguards must overlap the width of the tire tread, and the outer side edges of the mudguards must be rounded. The distance between the tire tread and the cover must be at least 60 mm, and between the tire sidewall and the cover at least 50 mm,

- b) for machines intended for construction and similar activities at the operating mass of the machine, the wheel covers must meet the requirements of technical standard ČSN EN ISO 3457.

- 10. Diesel engines powering machines must, in terms of exhaust gas emissions, comply with the conditions stipulated by ECE technical regulation No. 96, 97/68/EC, and must be approved according to these regulations.

- 11. The maximum sound pressure level must not exceed, under the measurement conditions stipulated by the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the approval of agricultural and forestry vehicles and market surveillance^13), a value of

85 dB (A) for machines with an operating mass up to 1.50 t,

89 dB (A) for machines with an operating mass exceeding 1.50 t.

The machine must be in the transport position according to the operating manual and with the selected working attachment connected during measurement.

- 12. The external lighting and light signaling devices of machines must comply with the conditions stipulated by technical standard ČSN ISO 12 509.

- 13. Machines must, in terms of electromagnetic compatibility, comply with the conditions stipulated by the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the approval of agricultural and forestry vehicles and market surveillance^13) or technical standards, ČSN EN ISO 14982, ISO 13766, ČSN EN 55012 ed.2.

- 14. Machines with a maximum design speed exceeding 6 km.h^-1 must have at least one device for a sufficiently loud audible warning. Machines must, in terms of audible warning devices and their signals, comply with the conditions stipulated by ECE technical regulation No. 28.

- 15. Machines with an operating mass exceeding 400 kg must be structurally adapted and designed at the front or rear so that they can be towed by other vehicles with a bar or rope for recovery or towing purposes.

- 16. Machines with a maximum design speed exceeding 40 km.h^-1 must be sufficiently sprung, with the possible use of shock absorbers and stabilizers.

- 17. For parts, components, assemblies, and groups used or intended for machines, materials containing asbestos must not be used.

- 18. The external surface of machines must not have pointed or sharp outward-facing protrusions that, by their shape, dimensions, or hardness, would increase the risk of injury to persons. Outward-facing parts that could snag other road users must be fitted with protective strips or similar devices forming a sufficient lead-in or guide approximately parallel to the longitudinal median plane of the machine.

- 19. Machines with a maximum permissible mass greater than 3.50 t must be equipped with at least one wheel chock. Machines with three or more axles must be equipped with at least two wheel chocks. These chocks must be capable of effectively securing the machine against unintentional movement, must be easily accessible to the operator, and safely graspable. The provisions of this article do not apply to machines that can be secured against unintentional movement by their own component.

- 20. The fuel tank must comply with the conditions stipulated in technical standards ČSN EN 474-1, ČSN EN ISO 4254-1.

- 21. Machines must be equipped with an exhaust pipe with an effective silencer for damping the noise of exhaust gases. The end part of the exhaust pipe must be directed upwards above the vehicle, or backwards, or to the left. The exhaust pipe outlet must be routed so as to prevent exhaust gases from entering the driver's cab - the driver's position. If the vehicle's exhaust pipe is routed vertically upwards, it must be fitted with a flap at the top or a drain valve at the lowest point.

- 22. Electrical equipment must comply with the conditions stipulated in technical standards ČSN EN 474-1, ISO 1724.

- 23. Machines whose maximum design speed does not exceed 40 km.h^-1, slow-moving vehicles, must be fitted with rear marking plates at the rear when operating on roads. This marking must comply with the conditions stipulated by ECE technical regulation No. 69 and must be approved accordingly.

- 24. Machines equipped with a driver's cab must be equipped with a ventilation system, possibly also a heating system, and at least one sun visor, with the exception of machines where the use of a visor is not compatible with their work activity, ensuring an upward view of the working equipment - such as loaders, telescopic handlers, etc. The sun visor must be implemented at least in the form of an approved self-adhesive film affixed to the cab's windshield. The driver's workplace and the driver's cab must comply with the conditions stipulated by technical regulations or technical standards ČSN EN 474-1, ČSN EN 500-1, ČSN EN ISO 2867 ed. 2, ČSN ISO 5006, ČSN ISO 10968, ČSN EN ISO 6682, ČSN ISO 6405-1, ČSN EN ISO 4254-1, ČSN ISO 4254-7, ČSN ISO 5721, ČSN EN ISO 3767-1, ECE regulations No. 43, ECE No. 71, or the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the approval of agricultural and forestry vehicles and market surveillance^13).

Technical requirements for mounted work machines of category SN

- 25. Mounted work machines are machines intended solely for performing specific work activities. These machines move on roads suspended on a vehicle and form a single unit with it. Their running gear, if equipped, is not in contact with the road surface in the transport position. This category also includes interchangeable superstructures having the character of a mounted work machine. For machines whose connection cannot be made via a standardized coupling device and requires intervention in the carrier's structure, the carrier manufacturer's consent to the installation must be provided.

- 26. For mounted work machines (hereinafter referred to as “machines”), the provisions of this Annex and the provisions of the Decree concerning

- a) vehicle dimensions,

- b) marking of vehicle outlines, and

- c) marking of certain data on the vehicle.

- 27. When operating on roads, the machine must be in the approved transport position, must not significantly reduce the driver's view from the vehicle, restrict vehicle maneuverability, or limit the visibility of its lighting devices.

- 28. The external lighting and light signaling devices of the machine must comply with the conditions stipulated for interchangeable towed machines, with the exception of the requirement for rear reflectors, which may also be non-triangular in shape.

- 29. Machines, with the exception of front-mounted ones, do not need to be equipped with their own external lighting and light signaling devices or holders for a portable set of these lighting devices under the following conditions:

- a) if the geometric visibility of the vehicle's lighting devices is not impaired,

- b) if the stipulated distance of the vehicle's external lighting and light signaling devices from the side outlines of the machine is not exceeded, and

- c) the length of the machine does not increase the length of the vehicle by more than 1.50 m.

- 30. Machines, with the exception of front-mounted ones, whose width does not exceed 2.00 m and are mounted symmetrically with the longitudinal axis of the vehicle, do not need to have the front part marked with an outline, front position lamps, and front reflectors.

- 31. Outline marking may be performed on double-sided removable shields placed in the middle third of the machine's length under the following conditions:

- a) that these shields are visible from both front and rear, and

- b) the length of the machine does not increase the length of the vehicle by more than 1.50 m.

- 32. A machine whose width in the transport position is substantially smaller than the width of the tractor-carrier, and compliance with the requirements for the mutual distance of the rear lighting devices would mean an increase in the machine's width, may be equipped at the rear with only one warning plate, one Class IIIa reflector, one left lamp with position, brake, and direction indicator light.

- 33. A side-mounted machine may only be attached to the right side of the vehicle.

- 34. The external surface of machines must not have pointed or sharp outward-facing protrusions that, by their shape, dimensions, or hardness, would increase the risk of injury to persons. Outward-facing parts that could snag other road users must be fitted with protective strips or similar devices forming a sufficient lead-in or guide approximately parallel to the longitudinal median plane of the machine.

- 35. The electrical installation of the machine must be insulated and routed so as to prevent accidental damage to conductors or the occurrence of a short circuit. The connection of the electrical installation for the prescribed lighting to the towing vehicle must be made with a seven-core electrical cable with a seven-pin plug of black - dark color. The plug, in terms of design, color, and placement, must comply with the conditions stipulated by technical standard ISO 1724 and must be approved accordingly.

- 36. Machines whose maximum permissible speed does not exceed 40 km.h^-1 must be fitted with rear marking plates at the rear when operating on roads. This marking must comply with the conditions stipulated by ECE technical regulation No. 69 and must be approved accordingly. The machine does not need to be equipped with this marking provided that this marking is placed on the vehicle and is not covered by the machine or its part.

Technical requirements for category Z vehicles

Single-axle tractors with trailers

- 37. A single-axle tractor is a vehicle with a driven axle, steerable by handlebars by a driver sitting on the trailer seat, with which it forms a vehicle combination. When steering, such a position of the tractor handlebars that would prevent simultaneous holding of both handles must not be possible. Operation of a single-axle tractor without a trailer on roads is prohibited.

- 38. For a single-axle tractor with a trailer, the provisions of this Annex and the provisions of the Decree concerning

- a) wheels and tires;

- b) marking of certain data on the vehicle;

- c) manufacturer's or factory plate, serial number.

- 39. The maximum design speed of the vehicle combination must not exceed 10 km.h^-1.

- 40. The vehicle combination must not exceed

- a) a total width of 1.60 m,

- b) a total length of 4.00 m, and

- c) a maximum permissible mass of 1.50 t.

- 41. The vehicle combination must be equipped with service and parking braking systems on at least one vehicle of the vehicle combination. These systems may share common parts. The parking braking system must be exclusively mechanical.

- 42. The value of the service braking effect of the vehicle combination, expressed as mean braking deceleration, must not be less than 1.6 m.s^-2. The parking braking effect must reliably hold a stationary vehicle combination on a slope, both uphill and downhill, with a gradient of at least 12%. Braking effects are evaluated at both the operating and maximum permissible mass of the vehicle combination.

The maximum permissible forces on the controls for both service and parking braking must not exceed

- a) 60 N - on a lever on the handlebars, operated by fingers;

- b) 250 N - on a lever operated by one hand;

- c) 400 N - on a pedal.

- 43. The trailer must be equipped with reflectors according to the requirements of the relevant provisions for category R vehicles.

- 44. If the vehicle combination is equipped with external lighting and light signaling devices, their design and placement on the trailer must comply with the requirements of the relevant provisions for category S vehicles; the tractor must be equipped with at least one headlamp with a dipped-beam light of an approved type placed in its longitudinal median plane, and one of the vehicles of the vehicle combination must be equipped with approved front direction indicator lamps, category 1, placed so that the horizontal angles of geometric visibility are 80 degrees outwards and 10 degrees inwards, and the vertical angles are 15 degrees above and below the horizontal. If the vehicle combination is not equipped with these devices, its operation on roads in reduced visibility is prohibited.

- 45. The external noise level must comply with the requirements stipulated for category T vehicles.

- 46. The exhaust pipe outlet of the tractor must be directed so as to prevent the driver from inhaling exhaust gases.

- 47. For a vehicle combination whose maximum permissible mass exceeds 400 kg, the tractor must be equipped with a reverse gear device - for reversing, which is controllable from the driver's seat.

- 48. The tractor must, in terms of electromagnetic compatibility, comply with the conditions stipulated in the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the approval of agricultural and forestry vehicles and market surveillance^13) or technical standards ČSN EN ISO 14982, or ISO 13766, ČSN EN 55012 ed.2.

- 49. The tractor's coupling device must secure the trailer against unintentional detachment with a double mechanical safety lock.

- 50. For parts, components, assemblies, and groups used or intended for vehicles, materials containing asbestos must not be used.

- 51. The external surface of vehicles must not have pointed or sharp outward-facing protrusions that, by their shape, dimensions, or hardness, would increase the risk of injury to persons.

- 52. The electrical installation must be insulated and routed so as to prevent accidental damage to conductors or the occurrence of a short circuit. The interconnection of the electrical installation between the tractor and the trailer must be made with a seven-core electrical cable with a seven-pin plug and socket of black - dark color. The plug and socket, in terms of design and color, must comply with the conditions stipulated by technical standard ISO 1724 and must be approved accordingly.

- 53. Machines must be fitted with rear marking plates for slow-moving vehicles at the rear when operating on roads. This marking must comply with the conditions stipulated by ECE technical regulation No. 69 and must be approved accordingly.

Special carriers for work attachments

- 54. Special carriers for work attachments are light motor vehicles intended for demanding terrain, collectively referred to in international terminology as ATV - All Terrain Vehicles, designed and equipped primarily for mounting various work attachments, or for transporting less bulky cargo, which may be operated on roads in necessary cases.

In some cases, they may be structurally derived from category L motor vehicles, e.g., special quad bikes.

They are not intended for passenger transport, but a person may be transported if required for the vehicle's work activity, or if it is necessary for loading and unloading cargo and if the vehicle is equipped with an appropriate passenger seat.

- 55. For special carriers for work attachments (hereinafter referred to as “vehicle”), the provisions of this Annex and, mutatis mutandis, the provisions of the Decree concerning:

- a) wheels and tires,

- b) marking of certain data on the vehicle,

- c) manufacturer's or factory plate, serial number,

- d) special warning light devices,

- e) dimensions and placement of the registration plate prescribed for category L vehicles, and

- f) mandatory equipment of the vehicle prescribed for category L vehicles.

- 56. The vehicle must not exceed

- a) a total width of: 2.0 m,

- b) a total length of: 4.0 m,

- c) a total height of: 2.5 m,

- d) a maximum permissible mass of: 1.0 t, and

- e) a maximum design speed of: 40 km.h^-1.

- 57. The maximum sound pressure level must not exceed, under the measurement conditions stipulated by the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the approval of agricultural and forestry vehicles and market surveillance^13), a value of 85 dB (A). The vehicle, even with any mounted work attachment, must be in the transport position according to the operating manual during measurement.

- 58. In terms of braking equipment, vehicles must comply with the requirements of the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the approval of agricultural and forestry vehicles and market surveillance^13) or ČSN EN ISO 3450.

If it is a vehicle structurally derived from category L vehicles, it must meet the following requirements:

Type 0 test:

- a) vehicles with braking devices acting only on the front or rear wheels:

The initial speed is equal to the maximum design speed of the vehicle.

For the calculation of braking distance when braking with only the front and only the rear brake, the following formulas apply:

- 1. with a loaded vehicle:

- 2. with an unloaded vehicle:

- b) vehicles having one of the braking devices which is a combined braking system within the meaning of the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the approval of two- or three-wheel vehicles and quadricycles and market surveillance^12).

The initial speed is equal to the maximum design speed of the vehicle.

For the calculation of braking distance when braking with a combined braking system with the vehicle in both loaded and unloaded condition, the following formula applies:

s=<0.1 v+v2115

For the calculation of braking distance when braking with the second service braking device or the emergency braking device within the meaning of technical regulation 93/14/EEC on the braking systems of two- and three-wheel motor vehicles, with the vehicle in both loaded and unloaded condition, the following formula applies:

s=<0.1 v+v265

The stated effects must also be achieved in a vehicle combination with a trailer.

For the formulas:

s = actual braking distance measured during the test

v = actual initial speed measured during the test

Type I test shall be performed according to the relevant provisions of technical regulation 93/14/EEC on the braking systems of two- and three-wheel motor vehicles, with the initial speed being equal to the maximum design speed of the vehicle.

The test of the parking braking effect in a vehicle combination with an unbraked trailer is performed at both the operating and maximum permissible mass of the vehicle.

- 59. The vehicle's steering must allow for smooth maintenance of the intended direction of travel. Vehicles equipped with a steering wheel must comply with the conditions stipulated in the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the approval of agricultural and forestry vehicles and market surveillance^13).

- 60. The maximum permissible forces on the vehicle controls must not exceed:

- a) 200 N - for levers located on the handlebars and operated only by fingers,

- b) 400 N - on the parking brake lever, 250 N - on other levers operated by one hand,

- c) 500 N - on the pedal,

- d) 250 N - on the steering wheel.

- 61. Vehicles must be equipped with a reverse gear device - for reversing, which is controllable from the driver's seat.

- 62. The external lighting and light signaling devices of vehicles must comply with the conditions stipulated by ECE technical regulations No. 86, and in the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the approval of agricultural and forestry vehicles and market surveillance^13).

For lighting devices where the technical regulation stipulates a requirement for the mutual distance of the inner edges of their active surfaces, this distance may be reduced to 400 mm in justified cases.

Lighting devices intended for category L vehicles may be used on vehicles.

Lighting devices must comply with the conditions stipulated by technical regulations and must be approved accordingly.

- 63. Vehicles must, in terms of radio interference suppression, comply with the conditions stipulated by the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the approval of agricultural and forestry vehicles and market surveillance^13) or technical standards ČSN EN ISO 14982, or ISO 13766.

- 64. Vehicles with a maximum design speed exceeding 6 km.h^-1 must have at least one audible warning device, which must comply with the requirements stipulated by ECE technical regulation No. 28 and must be approved accordingly; in terms of signals, it must comply with the conditions stipulated by ECE technical regulations No. 28, or the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the approval of agricultural and forestry vehicles and market surveillance^13).

- 65. In terms of maximum design speed, vehicles must comply with the requirements of technical regulation 2009/60/EC.

- 66. Vehicles with a maximum design speed greater than 20 km.h^-1 must be equipped with wheel covers, or mud flaps, which must comply with the following conditions:

- a) cover the entire width of the tire tread of all vehicle wheels, and

- b) by their shape and dimensions, ensure effective protection of the driver, controls, and indicators from dirt splashing from the wheels of the vehicle moving forward or backward.

- 67. Vehicles with an operating mass exceeding 400 kg must be structurally adapted at the front or rear so that they can be towed by other vehicles with a bar or rope for recovery or towing purposes.

- 68. Vehicles must be equipped with an exhaust pipe with an effective silencer for damping the noise of exhaust gases. The end part of the exhaust pipe must be directed upwards above the vehicle, or backwards, or to the left. The exhaust pipe outlet must be routed so as to prevent exhaust gases from entering the driver's cab, the driver's position. If the vehicle's exhaust pipe is routed vertically upwards, it must be fitted with a flap at the top or a drain valve at the lowest point.

- 69. The external surface of vehicles must not have pointed or sharp outward-facing protrusions that, by their shape, dimensions, or hardness, would increase the risk of injury to persons. Outward-facing parts that could snag other road users must be fitted with protective strips or similar devices forming a sufficient lead-in or guide approximately parallel to the longitudinal median plane of the machine.

- 70. The electrical installation of vehicles must be insulated and routed so as to prevent accidental damage to conductors or the occurrence of a short circuit. The connection of the electrical installation for the prescribed lighting of the trailer must be made with a seven-core electrical cable with a seven-pin socket of black - dark color. The socket, in terms of design, color, and placement, must comply with the conditions stipulated by technical standard ISO 1724 and must be approved accordingly.

- 71. Vehicles must be equipped with at least two external rearview mirrors, which must be approved according to the relevant technical regulations for mirrors of category M, N, T, or L vehicles.

In terms of driver's view, vehicles must comply with the requirements of technical regulation 2008/2/EC. Cargo or a mounted work attachment encroaching on the driver's field of vision must not significantly reduce visibility.

- 72. For parts, components, assemblies, and groups used on or intended for the vehicle, materials containing asbestos must not be used.

- 73. A trailer of an approved type may be attached to vehicles, whose instantaneous mass may be at most equal to the operating mass of the towing vehicle, unless otherwise stipulated when technical fitness is approved.

- 74. The mechanical coupling device for vehicles must be approved according to the relevant ECE technical regulations No. 55.

- 75. The driver's seat must be designed to ensure a comfortable position for steering and control, must be made of a material with thermal insulation properties, and must be upholstered. All controls must be comfortably reachable for the seated driver.

- 76. Vehicles must be equipped with suitable footrests for the driver, and possibly the passenger; the passenger's position must additionally be equipped with an appropriate handhold. Protection against moving and hot parts must meet at least the requirements stipulated in the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the approval of agricultural and forestry vehicles and market surveillance^13) for narrow-track tractors. Access to the driver's position must meet the requirements of the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the approval of agricultural and forestry vehicles and market surveillance^13).

- 77. The fuel tank must comply with the requirements of the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the approval of two- or three-wheel vehicles and quadricycles and market surveillance^12).

- 78. Machines must be fitted with rear marking plates for slow-moving vehicles at the rear when operating on roads. This marking must comply with the conditions stipulated by ECE technical regulation No. 69 and must be approved accordingly.

Trolleybuses and trams

- 79. For the purposes of this Decree, a trolleybus is a rail vehicle powered either by electric motors supplied from overhead lines, electric motors supplied from batteries in combination with overhead line supply, or an internal combustion engine with electric power transmission in combination with overhead line supply. This vehicle moves on roads along a so-called virtual track, the route of which is determined by the Rail Authority. A trolleybus is subject to another legal regulation^11). A trolleybus is, in terms of construction, a bus with a special type of propulsion, with specific requirements for some of its systems, structural parts, and separate technical units being stipulated by international agreements in the field of vehicle technical fitness approval, by which the Czech Republic is bound.

- 80. A tram is a rail vehicle powered by electric motors supplied from overhead lines, which moves on tracks located on roads. Trams are primarily subject to a special legal regulation^10) and only some provisions of this Decree, e.g., requirements for dimensions, and for direction indicator and brake lights.

- 81. The relevant international agreements in the field of technical fitness approval, by which the Czech Republic is bound, concerning systems, structural parts, and separate technical units of trolleybuses or trams, stipulate the method of verification, content, and arrangement of approval certificates for these systems, structural parts, and separate technical units of mass-produced trolleybuses and trams. If a system, structural part, and separate technical unit comply with the technical requirements stipulated in the international agreements in the field of technical fitness approval, by which the Czech Republic is bound, the Ministry shall issue an approval certificate for it.

Other vehicles

- 82. For the scope and content of documentation containing technical data on the type of category Z vehicles and subcategories SS and SN, the requirements stipulating the content of documentation in Annexes No. 3 to 6 shall be used mutatis mutandis.

- 83. For other category Z vehicles, the technical, harmonized technical, and international technical requirements of the vehicle categories from which the vehicle construction is derived shall be used mutatis mutandis.
